name: AACAudit
description: Azure App Configuration audit logs.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when log was generated.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: The log category of the event.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: GUID for correlated logs.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Status of this audit event.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the audited operation.

columns.name: ETag
columns.description: An identifier for a specific version of the resource.

columns.name: EventCategory
columns.description: Audit category of this event.

columns.name: TargetResource
columns.description: Target resource that apply to the operation being audited.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: The result of the operation being audited.

columns.name: CallerIdentity
columns.description: Caller identity of this event.

columns.name: CallerIPAddress
columns.description: IP Address of the caller that triggered this audit event.

columns.name: RequestId
columns.description: Unique request ID generated by server.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

name: AACHttpRequest
description: Incoming requests to Azure App Configuration. The records in this table are aggregated. The 'HitCount' field describes the number of requests that each record accounts for.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) marking the start of the aggregation period.

columns.name: Method
columns.description: HTTP Method.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: GUID for correlated logs.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: The log category of the event.

columns.name: RequestId
columns.description: Unique request ID generated by server.

columns.name: ClientRequestId
columns.description: Request ID provided by client.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: The average duration of the operation, in milliseconds, of all the requests included in this aggregated entry.

columns.name: RequestURI
columns.description: URI of the requests.

columns.name: ClientObjectId
columns.description: Object ID provided by client when authenticated via AAD.

columns.name: ClientTenantId
columns.description: Tenant ID provided by client when authenticated via AAD.

columns.name: AccessKeyId
columns.description: Access Key ID provided by client when authenticated via HMAC.

columns.name: ClientIPAddress
columns.description: IP Address of the client that sent the requests.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: User Agent provided by the client.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: HTTP Status Code of the requests.

columns.name: BytesSent
columns.description: The total number of bytes sent by the server across all requests included in this aggregated entry.

columns.name: BytesReceived
columns.description: The total number of bytes received by the server across all requests included in this aggregated entry.

columns.name: HitCount
columns.description: Count of requests that are included in this aggregated HTTP request log entry.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

name: AADB2CRequestLogs
description: Logs generated by AAD gateway for displaying B2C tenant's web requests and its throttle info.

columns.name: AADTenantId
columns.description: The AAD tenant ID of associated request.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Indicates whether the request is throttled or not.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: The HTTP status code of the corresponding REST call.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: Hashed format of caller IP address. Can be used to identify if a single IP address is using a large portion of traffic.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The date and time of the request received at the AAD gateway in UTC.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID for correlated log analytics events. Can be used to identify correlated events between multiple tables.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

name: AADCustomSecurityAttributeAuditLogs
description: Non-interactive Azure Active Directory sign-in logs from user.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: Details of source system of the object being provisioned.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The date and time of the event in UTC.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: The REST API version that's requested by the client.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: The result of the audit operation can be Success or Failure.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: Contains the error code, if any, for the audit operation.

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: Provides the error description for the audit operation.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: The duration of the operation in milliseconds.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: ID to provide audit trail.

columns.name: Identity
columns.description: The identity from the token that was presented when you made the request. It can be a user account, system account, or service principal.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: The severity level of the event.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The region of the resource emitting the event.

columns.name: AdditionalDetails
columns.description: Indicates additional details on the activity. Can have any string as a key or value.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: Unique ID representing the audit activity.

columns.name: InitiatedBy
columns.description: User or app initiated the activity.

columns.name: LoggedByService
columns.description: Service that initiated the activity.

columns.name: Result
columns.description: Result of the activity. Possible values are: success failure timeout unknownFutureValue.

columns.name: ResultReason
columns.description: Describes cause of failure or timeout results.

columns.name: TargetResources
columns.description: Indicates information on which resource was changed due to the activity.

columns.name: AADTenantId
columns.description: ID of the AAD tenant.

columns.name: ActivityDisplayName
columns.description: Activity name or the operation name.

columns.name: ActivityDateTime
columns.description: Date and time the activity was performed in UTC.

columns.name: AADOperationType
columns.description: Type of the operation. Possible values are Add Update Delete and Other.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: IP address of caller.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: User agent of initiator.

name: AADDomainServicesDNSAuditsGeneral
description: DNS server audit events enable change tracking on the DNS server. An audit event is logged each time server, zone, or resource record settings are changed. This includes operational events such as zone transfers, and DNSSEC zone signing and unsigning.  This table captures audit events that are not from dynamic updates.

name: ACSCallSummary
description: Call summary logs provide an overview about a call made through ACS. There is one log for every participant in the call, and logs contain information about the duration of the call, the duration of the individual participant, the type of participant (e.g. VoIP, PSTN, etc.), as well as the endpoint information like the OS version being used, or the SDK version of the ACS platform.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the log was generated.

columns.name: OperationName

columns.name: Category

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID for correlated events. Can be used to identify correlated events between multiple tables.

columns.name: Identifier
columns.description: The indentifier of the call used to correlate. Can be used to identify correlated events between multiple tables.

columns.name: CallStartTime
columns.description: Start time of the call.

columns.name: CallDuration
columns.description: Duration of the call in seconds.

columns.name: CallType
columns.description: Type of the call, for example P2P (peer to peer).

columns.name: TeamsThreadId
columns.description: Thread ID of the team.

columns.name: ParticipantId
columns.description: ID of the participant.

columns.name: ParticipantTenantId
columns.description: The ID of the Microsoft tenant associated with the identity of the participant. The tenant can either be the Azure tenant that owns the ACS resource or the Microsoft tenant of an M365 identity. This field is used to guide cross-tenant redaction.

columns.name: ParticipantStartTime
columns.description: Start time of the participant.

columns.name: ParticipantEndReason

columns.name: EndpointType
columns.description: Type of the endpoint, for example VoIP (voice over IP).

columns.name: OsVersion
columns.description: Operating System version.

columns.name: PstnParticipantCallType
columns.description: The type and direction of PSTN participants, including emergency calling, direct routing, transfer, forwarding, etc.

columns.name: ParticipantType
columns.description: Description of the participant as a combination of its client (Azure Communication Services (ACS) or Teams), and its identity, (ACS or Microsoft 365). columns.description: The Id of the experiment scorecard.

columns.description: The display name of the metric.

columns.description: The description of the metric.

columns.name: MetricKind
columns.description: The metric kind. Possible values include EventCount, UserCount, EventRate, UserRate, Sum, Average, Percentile.

columns.name: MetricLifecycle
columns.description: The lifecycle stage of the metric. Possible values include Active, Inactive

columns.name: MetricTags
columns.description: Metric tags in the form of string array.

columns.name: DesiredDirection
columns.description: Desirable direction for the metric. Possible values: Increase, Decrease, Neutral

columns.name: MetricVersion
columns.description: The version of the metric.

columns.name: MetricETag
columns.description: The ETag of the metric.

columns.name: TreatmentVariant
columns.description: The Id of the treatment variant.

columns.name: TreatmentCount
columns.description: The sample count of the treatment variant.

columns.name: TreatmentMetricValue
columns.description: The metric value for the treatment variant.

columns.name: TreatmentMetricValueNormalized
columns.description: The normalized metric value for the treatment variant. Used by metric comparisons, which accounts for unequal traffic allocation.

columns.name: TreatmentStandardErrorNormalized
columns.description: The standard error (StandardDeviation / sqrt(Count)) of the metric for the control variant.

columns.name: ControlVariant
columns.description: The Id of the control variant.

columns.name: ControlCount
columns.description: The sample count of the control variant.

columns.name: ControlMetricValue
columns.description: The metric value for the control variant.

columns.name: ControlMetricValueNormalized
columns.description: The normalized metric value for the control variant. Used by metric comparisons, which accounts for unequal traffic allocation.

columns.name: ControlStandardErrorNormalized
columns.description: The standard error (StandardDeviation / sqrt(Count)) of the metric for the control variant.

columns.name: PValue
columns.description: The P-Value of the comparison. Used to indicate if the difference between the variants is significant.

columns.name: TreatmentEffect
columns.description: The effect of the treatment variant on the metric. Possible values: Zero samples, Too few samples, Inconclusive, Changed, Improved, Degraded.

columns.name: RelativeDifference
columns.description: The relative difference of the comparison based on TreatmentMetricValueNormalized and ControlMetricValueNormalized.

name: AGCAccessLogs
description: Contains details of client requests made to Application Gateway for Containers. Each client request creats a log entry that can be used to identify slow requests, determine error rates, and correlate logs with backend services.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Time (UTC) when the log was created.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The region where Application Gateway for Containers association is deployed

columns.name: BackendHost
columns.description: Address of backend target with appended port. For example <ip>:<port>

columns.name: BackendIp
columns.description: IP address of backend target Application Gateway for Containers proxies the request to.

columns.name: BackendPort
columns.description: Port number of the backend target.

columns.name: BackendResponseLatency
columns.description: Time in milliseconds to receive first byte from Application Gateway for Containers to the backend target.

columns.name: BackendTimeTaken
columns.description: Time in milliseconds for the response to be transmitted from the backend target to Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: ClientIp
columns.description: IP address of the client initiating the request to the frontend of Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: FrontendName
columns.description: Name of the Application Gateway for Containers frontend that received the request from the client.

columns.name: FrontendPort
columns.description: Port number the request was listened on by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: Host header value received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: HttpMethod
columns.description: HTTP Method of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers as per RFC 7231.

columns.name: HttpStatusCode
columns.description: HTTP Status code returned from Application Gateway for Containers to the client.

columns.name: HttpVersion
columns.description: HTTP version of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: Referrer
columns.description: Referrer header of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: RequestBodyBytes
columns.description: Size in bytes of the body payload of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: RequestHeaderBytes
columns.description: Size in bytes of the headers of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: RequestUri
columns.description: URI of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers (everything after <protocol>://<host> of the URL).

columns.name: ResponseBodyBytes
columns.description: Size in bytes of the body payload of the response returned to the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: ResponseHeaderBytes
columns.description: Size in bytes of the headers of the response returned to the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: TimeTaken
columns.description: Time in milliseconds of the client request received by Application Gateway for Containers and the last byte returned to the client from Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: TlsCipher
columns.description: TLS cipher suite negotiated between the client and Application Gateway for Containers frontend.

columns.name: TlsProtocol
columns.description: TLS version negotiated between the client and Application Gateway for Containers frontend.

columns.name: TrackingId
columns.description: Generated guid by Application Gateway for Containers to help with tracking and debugging. This value correlates to the x-request-id header returned to the client from Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: User-Agent header of the request received from the client by Application Gateway for Containers.

columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: ClientPort

columns.description: Action taken on the request. Available values are Blocked and Allowed (for custom rules), Matched (when a rule matches a part of the request), and Detected and Blocked (these are both for mandatory rules, depending on if the WAF is in detection or prevention mode).

columns.name: DetailedMessage

columns.name: DetailedData

columns.name: FileDetails
columns.description: Configuration file that contained the rule for the triggered event.

columns.name: LineDetails
columns.description: Line number in the configuration file that triggered the event.

columns.name: Hostname

columns.name: PolicyId
columns.description: Resource ID of the web application firewall policy.

columns.name: PolicyScope
columns.description: A named scope consisting of Kubernetes resource references the scope is applied to.

columns.name: PolicyScopeName
columns.description: The name to the type of scope assignment the web application firewall policy is assigned to.

columns.name: RequestUri
columns.description: URL of the received request.

columns.name: RuleSetType
columns.description: Rule set type. The available value is Microsoft_DefaultRuleSet or Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet.

columns.name: RuleSetVersion
columns.description: Rule set version used for Microsoft_DefaultRuleSet or Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet.

columns.name: RuleId
columns.description: Rule ID of the triggering event.

columns.name: TrackingId
columns.description: Generated guid by Application Gateway for Containers to help with tracking and debugging. This value correlates to the x-request-id header returned to the client from Application Gateway for Containers.

name: AGWAccessLogs
description: Contains all the log to view Application Gateway access patterns and analyze important information. This includes the caller's IP, requested URL, response latency, return code, and bytes in and out.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Time (UTC) when the log was created.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: ListenerName
columns.description: The listener associated with given request log.

columns.name: RuleName
columns.description: The rule associated with the given request log.

columns.name: BackendPoolName
columns.description: The backend pool associated with the given request log.

columns.name: BackendSettingName
columns.description: The backend setting name associated with the given request log.

columns.name: InstanceId
columns.description: Application Gateway instance that served the request.

columns.name: ClientIp
columns.description: IP of the immediate client of Application Gateway. If another proxy fronts your application gateway, this displays the IP of that fronting proxy.

columns.name: ClientPort
columns.description: Port of the client that initiated the request.

columns.name: HttpMethod
columns.description: HTTP method used by the request.

columns.name: RequestUri
columns.description: URI of the received request.

columns.name: RequestQuery
columns.description: Query string portion of the request URI.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: User agent from the HTTP request header.

columns.name: HttpStatus
columns.description: HTTP status code returned to the client from Application Gateway.

columns.name: HttpVersion
columns.description: HTTP version of the request.

columns.name: ReceivedBytes
columns.description: Size of packet received, in bytes.

columns.name: SentBytes
columns.description: Size of packet sent, in bytes.

columns.name: ClientResponseTime
columns.description: Time difference (in seconds) between first byte received from the backend to first byte sent to the client.

columns.name: TimeTaken
columns.description: Length of time (in seconds) that it takes for the first byte of a client request to be processed and its last-byte sent in the response to the client. It's important to note that the Time-Taken field usually includes the time that the request and response packets are traveling over the network.

columns.name: WafEvaluationTime
columns.description: Length of time (in seconds) that it takes for the request to be processed by the WAF.

columns.name: WafMode
columns.description: Value can be either Detection or Prevention.

columns.name: WafPolicyId
columns.description: The resource ID of the WAF policy associated with the Application Gateway.

columns.name: TransactionId
columns.description: Unique identifier to correlate the request received from the client.

columns.name: SslEnabled
columns.description: Whether communication to the backend pools used TLS. Valid values are on and off.

columns.name: SslCipher
columns.description: Cipher suite being used for TLS communication (if TLS is enabled).

columns.name: SslProtocol
columns.description: SSL/TLS protocol being used (if TLS is enabled).

columns.name: SslClientVerify
columns.description: Status of SSL client certificate verification.

columns.name: SslClientCertificateFingerprint
columns.description: Fingerprint of the SSL client certificate.

columns.name: SslClientCertificateIssuerName
columns.description: Issuer name of the SSL client certificate.

columns.name: ServerRouted
columns.description: The backend server that application gateway routes the request to.

columns.name: ServerStatus
columns.description: HTTP status code of the backend server.

columns.name: ServerResponseLatency
columns.description: Latency of the response (in seconds) from the backend server.

columns.name: ServerConnectTime
columns.description: Time (in seconds) taken to establish a connection with the backend server.

columns.name: ServerHeaderTime
columns.description: Time (in seconds) taken to receive the response headers from the backend server.

columns.name: BackendSslProtocol
columns.description: SSL/TLS protocol used for communication with the backend server.

columns.name: BackendSslCipher
columns.description: Cipher suite used for TLS communication with the backend server.

columns.name: ErrorInfo
columns.description: Error category of the failing request.

columns.name: Host
columns.description: Address listed in the host header of the request. If rewritten using header rewrite, this field contains the updated host name.

columns.name: OriginalRequestUriWithArgs
columns.description: This field contains the original request URL.

columns.name: UpstreamSourcePort
columns.description: The source port used by Application Gateway when initiating a connection to the backend target.

columns.name: OriginalHost
columns.description: This field contains the original request host name.

columns.description: Action taken on the request. Available values are Blocked and Allowed (for custom rules), Matched (when a rule matches a part of the request), and Detected and Blocked (these are both for mandatory rules, depending on if the WAF is in detection or prevention mode).

columns.description: The type of authentication that was used to make the request.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP status code for the request. If the request is interrupted, this value might be set to Unknown.

columns.name: StatusText
columns.description: The status of the requested operation.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: The total time, expressed in milliseconds, to perform the requested operation. This includes the time to read the incoming request, and to send the response to the requester.

columns.name: ServerLatencyMs
columns.description: The total time expressed in milliseconds to perform the requested operation. This value doesn't include network latency (the time to read the incoming request and send the response to the requester).

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Uniform resource identifier that is requested.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the requester, including the port number.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID that is used to correlate logs across resources.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: The schema version of the log.

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: The storage service version that was specified when the request was made. This is equivalent to the value of the x-ms-version header.

columns.name: AuthenticationHash
columns.description: The hash of authentication token.

columns.name: RequesterObjectId
columns.description: The OAuth object ID of the requester.

columns.name: RequesterTenantId
columns.description: The OAuth tenant ID of identity.

columns.name: RequesterAppId
columns.description: The Open Authorization (OAuth) application ID that is used as the requester.

columns.name: RequesterAudience
columns.description: The OAuth audience of the request.

columns.name: RequesterTokenIssuer
columns.description: The OAuth token issuer.

columns.name: RequesterUpn
columns.description: The User Principal Names of requestor.

columns.name: AuthorizationDetails
columns.description: Detailed policy information used to authorize the request.

columns.name: UserAgentHeader
columns.description: The User-Agent header value, in quotes.

columns.name: ReferrerHeader
columns.description: The Referer header value.

columns.name: ClientRequestId
columns.description: The x-ms-client-request-id header value of the request.

columns.name: Etag
columns.description: The ETag identifier for the returned object, in quotes.

columns.name: ServiceType
columns.description: The service associated with this request.

columns.name: OperationCount
columns.description: The number of each logged operation that is involved in the request. This count starts with an index of 0. Some requests require more than one operation, such as a request to copy a blob. Most requests perform only one operation.

columns.name: ObjectKey
columns.description: The key of the requested object, in quotes.

columns.name: RequestHeaderSize
columns.description: The size of the request header expressed in bytes. If a request is unsuccessful, this value might be empty.

columns.name: RequestBodySize
columns.description: The size of the request packets, expressed in bytes, that are read by the storage service. If a request is unsuccessful, this value might be empty.

columns.name: ResponseHeaderSize
columns.description: The size of the response header expressed in bytes. If a request is unsuccessful, this value might be empty.

columns.name: ResponseBodySize
columns.description: The size of the response packets written by the storage service, in bytes. If a request is unsuccessful, this value may be empty.

columns.name: RequestMd5
columns.description: The value of either the Content-MD5 header or the x-ms-content-md5 header in the request. The MD5 hash value specified in this field represents the content in the request.

columns.name: ResponseMd5
columns.description: The value of the MD5 hash calculated by the storage service.

columns.name: LastModifiedTime
columns.description: The Last Modified Time (LMT) for the returned object. This field is empty for operations that can return multiple objects.

columns.name: ConditionsUsed
columns.description: A semicolon-separated list of key-value pairs that represent a condition.

columns.name: ContentLengthHeader
columns.description: The value of the Content-Length header for the request sent to the storage service.

columns.name: TlsVersion
columns.description: The TLS version used in the connection of request.

columns.name: SasExpiryStatus
columns.description: Records any violations in the request SAS token as per the SAS policy set in the storage account. Ex: longer SAS token duration specified than allowed per SAS policy.

columns.name: MetricResponseType

columns.description: Records the source URI for operations.

columns.name: DestinationUri
columns.description: Records the destination URI for operations.

columns.name: AccessTier
columns.description: The access tier of the storage account.

columns.name: SourceAccessTier
columns.description: The source tier of the storage account.

columns.name: RehydratePriority
columns.description: The priority used to rehydrate an archived blob.

name: ASRJobs
description: This table contains records of Azure Site Recovery (ASR) jobs such as failover, test failover, reprotection etc., with key details for monitoring and diagnostics, such as the replicated item information, duration, status, description and so on. Whenever an ASR job is completed (i.e., succeeded or failed), a corresponding record for the job is sent to this table. You can view history of ASR jobs by querying this table over a larger time range, provided your workspace has the required retention configured.

name: ASRReplicatedItems
description: This table contains details of Azure Site Recovery (ASR) replicated items, such as associated vault, policy, replication health, failover readiness. etc. Data is pushed once a day to this table for all replicated items, to provide the latest information for each item.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) when the log was generated.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: The category of the log.

columns.name: VaultType
columns.description: Type of the vault associated with the replicated item.

columns.name: VaultName
columns.description: Name of the vault associated with the replicated item.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The name of the operation.

columns.name: ReplicationHealthErrors
columns.description: List of issues that might be affecting the recovery point generation for the replicated item.

columns.name: RecoveryRegion
columns.description: Target region to which the resource is replicated.

columns.name: MultiVMGroupId
columns.description: For scenarios where multi-VM consistency feature is enabled for replicated VMs, this field specifies the ID of the multi-VM group associated with the replicated VM.

columns.name: LastSuccessfulTestFailoverTime
columns.description: Time of the last successful faliover performed on the replicated item.

columns.name: LastHeartbeat
columns.description: Time at which the ASR agent associated with the replicated item last made a call to the ASR service. Useful for debugging error scenarios where you wish to identify the time at which issues started arising.

columns.name: ReplicationStatus
columns.description: Status of replication for the ASR replicated item.

columns.name: FailoverReadiness
columns.description: Denotes whether there are any configuration issues that could affect the failover operation success for the ASR replicated item.

columns.name: DatasourceFriendlyName
columns.description: Friendly name of the datasource being replicated.

columns.name: DatasourceUniqueId
columns.description: Unique ID of the datasource being replicated.

columns.name: PrimaryFabricName
columns.description: Represents the source region of the replicated item. By default, the value is the name of the source region, however if you have specified a custom name for the primary fabric while enabling replication, then that custom name shows up under this field.

columns.name: RecoveryFabricName
columns.description: Represents the target region of the replicated item. By default, the value is the name of the target region, however if you have specified a custom name for the recovery fabric while enabling replication, then that custom name shows up under this field.

columns.name: PrimaryFabricType
columns.description: Fabric type associated with the source region of the replicated item. Depending on whether the replicated item is an Azure VM, Hyper-V VM or VMWare VM, the value for this field varies.

columns.name: RecoveryFabricType
columns.description: Fabric type associated with the target region of the replicated item. Depending on whether the replicated item is an Azure VM, Hyper-V VM or VMWare VM, the value for this field varies.

columns.name: ProtectionInfo
columns.description: Protection status of the replicated item.

columns.name: ActiveLocation
columns.description: Current active location for the replicated item. If the item is in failed over state, the active location will be the secondary (target) region. Otherwise, it will be the primary region.

columns.name: PolicyFriendlyName
columns.description: Friendly name of the replication policy applied to the replicated item.

columns.name: OSFamily
columns.description: OS family of the resource being replicated.

columns.name: IRProgressPercentage
columns.description: Progress percentage of the initial replication phase for the replicated item.

columns.name: DatasourceType
columns.description: ARM type of the resource configured for replication.

columns.name: LastRpoCalculatedTime
columns.description: Time at which the RPO was last calculated by the ASR service for the replicated item.

columns.name: Version
columns.description: The API version.

columns.name: ReplicatedItemId
columns.description: ARM ID of the replicated item.

columns.name: ReplicatedItemUniqueId
columns.description: Unique ID of the replicated item.

columns.description: Unique ID of the replication policy applied for the replicated item.

columns.name: PolicyId

columns.description: Friendly name of the resource being replicated.

columns.description: Location of the vault associated with the replicated item.

name: ASRv2JobEvents
description: This table contains records of Azure Site Recovery v2 (ASRv2) jobs such as replication, migration, etc., with key details for monitoring and diagnostics, such as protected item's information, duration, status, description and so on. Whenever an ASRv2 job is completed (i.e., succeeded or failed), a corresponding record for the job is sent to this table. You can view history of ASRv2 jobs by querying this table over a larger time range, provided your workspace has the required retention configured.

columns.description: Identifies the type of message. For example, a firewall might use MSGID "TCPIN" for incoming traffic, and MSGID "TCPOUT" for outgoing traffic. Messages with the same MSGID reflect events of the same semantics.

name: AVSNsxEdgeSyslog
description: Contains logs generated by NSX Edges.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time at which the log was ingested.

columns.name: LogCreationTime
columns.description: The time at which the log was created, if available.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: The entire syslog message.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: The severity of the log. Acceptable values, in ascending level of severity: debug, info, notice, warn, err, crit, alert, emerg. This field may be empty.

columns.name: Facility
columns.description: Indicates the source that generated the syslog (e.g., an operating system, a process, an application, etc).

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: The name of the host that generated this log, if available.

columns.name: AppName
columns.description: The name of the application that generated this log, if available.

columns.name: ProcId
columns.description: Identifies a process; specific usage varies from application to application.

columns.name: MsgId
columns.description: Identifies the type of message. For example, a firewall might use MSGID "TCPIN" for incoming traffic, and MSGID "TCPOUT" for outgoing traffic. Messages with the same MSGID reflect events of the same semantics.

name: AZFWApplicationRule
description: Contains all Application rule log data. Each match between data plane and Application rule creates a log entry with the data plane packet and the matched rule's attributes.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the data plane log was created.

columns.name: Protocol
columns.description: Request's network protocol. For example: HTTP, HTTPS.

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: Request's source IP address.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: Request's source port.

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: Request's destination port.

columns.name: Fqdn
columns.description: Request's target address in FQDN (Fully qualified Domain Name). For example: www.microsoft.com.

columns.name: TargetUrl
columns.description: Request's target address URL. Available only for HTTP or TLS-inspected HTTPS requests. For example: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/about.

columns.name: Action
columns.description: Action taken by the firewall following the Application rule hit.

columns.name: Policy
columns.description: Name of the policy in which the triggered rule resides.

columns.name: RuleCollectionGroup
columns.description: Name of the rule collection group in which the triggered rule resides.

columns.name: RuleCollection
columns.description: Name of the rule collection in which the triggered rule resides.

columns.name: Rule
columns.description: Name of the triggered rule.

columns.name: ActionReason
columns.description: When no rule is triggered for a request, this field contains the reason for the action performed by the firewall. For example: a packet dropped because no rule matched will show `Default Action`.

columns.name: IsTlsInspected
columns.description: True if the connection is TLS inspected. False otherwise.

columns.name: WebCategory
columns.description: Web Category identified for the requested FQDN (Azure Firewall Standard) or URL (Azure Firewall Premium). If a web category is not available for this request, the field is empty.

columns.name: IsExplicitProxyRequest
columns.description: True if the request is received on an explicit proxy port. False otherwise.

columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

name: AZFWDnsFlowTrace
description: Contains all the DNS proxy data between the client, firewall, and DNS server.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the data plane log was created.

columns.name: MsgType
columns.description: Shows whether the DNS message is a query or response, and whether it's from the client or forwarded by the firewall.

columns.name: QueryTime
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the DNS query was initiated.

columns.name: ResponseTime
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the DNS response was received.

columns.name: SocketFamily
columns.description: Internet protocol family for the DNS query.

columns.name: Protocol
columns.description: Internet protocol used for the DNS query (e.g., TCP, UDP).

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: IP address of the source that initiated the DNS query.

columns.name: ServerIp
columns.description: IP address of the DNS server that responded.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: Source port from which the DNS query was sent.

columns.name: ServerPort
columns.description: Port on the DNS server that responded to the query.

columns.name: QueryMessage
columns.description: Details of the DNS query, including the FQDN or URL.

columns.name: ServerMessage
columns.description: Details of the DNS response received from the upstream DNS server.

columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the data plane log was created.

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: DNS query's source IP address.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: DNS query's source Port.

columns.name: QueryId
columns.description: DNS query's query ID.

columns.name: QueryType
columns.description: DNS query's query type.

columns.name: QueryClass
columns.description: DNS query's query class.

columns.name: QueryName
columns.description: DNS query's name to resolve.

columns.name: Protocol
columns.description: Protocol used to send the DNS query. For example: TCP, UDP.

columns.name: RequestSize
columns.description: The size of the DNS request in bytes.

columns.name: DnssecOkBit
columns.description: A flag indicating that the resolver supports DNSSEC records.

columns.name: EDNS0BufferSize
columns.description: Client's EDNS0 buffer size. Specifies the maximum packet size allowed in responses in bytes.

columns.name: ResponseCode
columns.description: DNS reponse code.

columns.name: ResponseFlags
columns.description: DNS reponse flags, comma separated.

columns.name: ResponseSize
columns.description: DNS reponse syze in bytes.

columns.name: RequestDurationSecs
columns.description: Duration of the DNS request from the time it arrived to the firewall and until a response was sent to the client.

columns.name: ErrorNumber
columns.description: Error number matching the returned response code.

columns.name: ErrorMessage
columns.description: Description of the error returned to the client. Empty if request is successful.

name: AZKVAuditLogs
description: Audit logs can be used to monitor how and when your key vaults are accessed, and by whom. Customers will be able to log all authentication api requests. Operations on the key vault itself, including creation, deletion, setting key vault access policies, and updating key vault attributes such as tags.Operation on keys and secrets in keyvault including creating, deleting, signing.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) when operation occured.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Result of the REST API request.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: Additional description about the result, when available.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: An optional GUID that the client can pass to correlate client-side logs with service-side (Key Vault) logs.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: IP address of the client that made the request

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: REST api version requested by the client.

columns.name: Identity
columns.description: Identity from the token that was presented in the REST API request. This is usually a user, a service principal, or the combination user+appId, as in the case of a request that results from an Azure PowerShell cmdlet.

columns.name: Properties
columns.description: Information that varies based on the operation (Operationname). In most cases, this field contains client information (the user agent string passed by the client), the exact REST API request URI, and the HTTP status code. In addition, when an object is returned as a result of a request (for example, KeyCreate or VaultGet), it also contains the key URI (as id), vault URI, or secret URI.

columns.name: Nsp
columns.description: Network security perimeter properties including access control list, nsp id's associated with profiles.

columns.name: KeyProperties
columns.description: Information about key properties including type, size, curve

columns.name: SecretProperties
columns.description: Information about secret properties including type, atttributes

columns.name: CertificateProperties
columns.description: Information about certificate audit properties including atttributes, subject, hashing algorithm

columns.name: CertificatePolicyProperties
columns.description: Information about certificate policy properties including keyproperties, secretproperties, issuerproperties

columns.name: CertificateIssuerProperties
columns.description: Information about certificate issuer properties including provider, id

columns.name: CertificateRequestProperties
columns.description: Boolean value indicating if certificate request operation was cancelled

columns.name: StorageAccountProperties
columns.description: Information about storage account properties including activekeyname, resourceid

columns.name: StorageSasDefinitionProperties
columns.description: Information about storage sas definition properties including sastype, validityperiod

columns.name: Id
columns.description: Resourceidentifier (Key ID or secret ID)

columns.name: Algorithm
columns.description: Algorithm used to generate the key

columns.name: ClientInfo
columns.description: User agent information

columns.name: SubnetId
columns.description: Id of subnet if request comes from a known subnet

columns.name: HttpStatusCode
columns.description: HTTP status code of the request

columns.name: RequestUri
columns.description: URI of the request

columns.name: IsAddressAuthorized
columns.description: Specifies whether request came from an authorized entity

columns.name: AddressAuthorizationType
columns.description: Address type (Public IP, subnet, private connection)

columns.name: IsAccessPolicyMatch
columns.description: True if the tenant matches vault tenant, and if the policy explicitly gives permission to the principal attempting the access.

columns.name: IsRbacAuthorized
columns.description: Specifies whether an access was granted or not as part of an access check

columns.name: AppliedAssignmentId
columns.description: AssignmentId that eiher granted or denied access as part of access check

columns.name: TrustedService
columns.description: Specifies whether the principal access the service is a trusted Service. If this field is null, principal is not a trusted service

columns.name: Tlsversion
columns.description: Network crypto protocol

columns.name: VaultProperties
columns.description: Detailed vault properties containing accesspolicy, iprule, virtualnetwork etc

name: AegDataPlaneRequests
description: Logs for Event Grid data plane requests (publish and options) against a topic/domain/partnernamespace. It can be used for auditing purposes. Logs are aggregated over a minute and displays the total number of requests with specific request properties.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the log was generated.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The name of the operation.

columns.name: NetworkAccess
columns.description: The type of network used by the client issuing the request. Allowed values are: PublicAccess - when connecting via public IP, PrivateAccess - when connecting via private link

columns.name: ClientIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the client issuing the request.

columns.name: TlsVersion
columns.description: The transport layer security (TLS) version used by the client connection. Possible values are: 1.0, 1.1 and 1.2

columns.name: Authentication
columns.description: The type of secret used for authentication when issuing requests. Key – request uses the SAS key, SASToken – request uses a SAS token generated from SAS key, AADAccessToken – Azure Active Directory issued JSON Web Token (JWT) token, Unknown – None of the above authentication types. OPTIONS requests will have Unknown authentication type.

columns.name: OperationResult
columns.description: Thw result of the operation. Possible values are: Success, Unauthorized, Forbidden, RequestEntityTooLarge, BadRequest, InternalServerError

columns.name: TotalOperations
columns.description: The total number of request with above values issued within the minute. These traces aren't emitted for each publish request. An aggregate for each unique combination of above values is emitted every minute

columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.description: The ingestion request ID used for identifying the message and the request for diagnostics and debugging.

columns.description: The name of the component that published this message. Almost always will be the name of the plugin, but can also be platform.

columns.name: PayloadType
columns.description: The type of the payload. Mostly used to distinguish between messages meant for different types of security analysis.

columns.name: Payload
columns.description: An array of messages, where each one is a JSON string.

columns.name: Index
columns.description: Available when multiple payloads exist for the same message. In that case, payloads share the same SlSecRequestId and Index defines the chronological order of payloads.

columns.name: SlSecMetadata
columns.description: Contains details about the resource like the deployment ID, runtime info, website info, OS, etc.

columns.name: SlSecProps
columns.description: Contains other details that might be needed for debugging end-to-end requests, e.g., slsec nuget version.

columns.name: MsgVersion
columns.description: The version of the message schema. Used to make code changes backward- and forward- compatible.

name: ArcK8sAudit
description: Contains all Kubernetes API Server audit logs including events with the get and list verbs. These events are useful for monitoring all of the interactions with the Kubernetes API. To limit the scope to modifying operations see the ArcK8sAuditAdmin table. Requires Diagnostic Settings to use the Resource Specific destination table.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Event generation time.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Level (Metadata, Request, RequestResponse) of the audit event.

columns.name: AuditId
columns.description: Unique audit ID that is generated for each request.

columns.name: Stage
columns.description: The request handling stage (RequestReceived, ResponseStarted, ResponseComplete, Panic) at which this audit event was generated.

columns.name: RequestUri
columns.description: The URI of the request made by the client to the server.

columns.name: Verb
columns.description: The Kubernetes verb associated with the request. For non-resource requests, this is the lower-cased HTTP method.

columns.name: User
columns.description: Authenticated user metadata of the requesting client, including optional fields such as UID and groups.

columns.name: SourceIps
columns.description: The list of source IP addresses for the originating client and intermediate proxies.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The user agent string presented by the originating client.

columns.name: ObjectRef
columns.description: The Kubernetes object reference this event was targeted for. This field does not apply for list requests nor non-resource requests.

columns.name: ResponseStatus
columns.description: Response status for the request, which includes the response code. In error cases, this object will include the error message property.

columns.name: RequestObject
columns.description: Kubernetes API object from the request in object format or the string "skipped-too-big-size-object". This is omitted for non-resource requests.

columns.name: ResponseObject
columns.description: Kubernetes API object from the response, in object format or the string "skipped-too-big-size-object". This is omitted for non-resource requests.

columns.name: RequestReceivedTime
columns.description: Time when the API Server first received the request.

columns.name: StageReceivedTime
columns.description: Time when the request reached the current audit stage.

columns.name: Annotations
columns.description: An unstructed key-value map associated with this audit event. These annotations are set by plugins as part of the request serving chain and are included at the Metadata event level.

columns.name: PodName
columns.description: Name of the pod emitting this audit event.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

name: AuditLogs
description: Audit log for Azure Active Directory. Includes system activity information about user and group management managed applications and directory activities.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the record was created.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: REST API version that's requested by the client.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Currently Audit is the only supported value.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Result of the operation. Possible values are Success and Failure.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: Property is not used and can be ignored.

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: Additional description of the result.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Property is not used and can be ignored.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: Optional GUID that's passed by the client. Can help correlate client-side operations with server-side operations and is useful when tracking logs that span services.

columns.name: Identity
columns.description: Identity from the token that was presented when the request was made. The identity can be a user account system account or service principal.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Message type. This is currently always Informational.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Location of the datacenter.

columns.name: AdditionalDetails
columns.description: Indicates additional details on the activity.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: GUID that uniquely identifies the activity.

columns.name: InitiatedBy
columns.description: User or app initiated the activity.

columns.name: LoggedByService
columns.description: Service that initiated the activity (For example: Self-service Password Management Core Directory B2C Invited Users Microsoft Identity Manager Privileged Identity Management.

columns.name: Result
columns.description: Result of the activity. Possible values are: success failure timeout unknownFutureValue.

columns.name: ResultReason
columns.description: Describes cause of failure or timeout results.

columns.name: TargetResources
columns.description: Indicates information on which resource was changed due to the activity. Target Resource Type can be User Device Directory App Role Group Policy or Other.

columns.name: AADTenantId
columns.description: ID of the ADD tenant

columns.name: ActivityDisplayName
columns.description: Activity name or the operation name. Examples include Create User and Add member to group. For full list see Azure AD activity list.

columns.name: ActivityDateTime
columns.description: Date and time the activity was performed in UTC.

columns.name: AADOperationType
columns.description: Type of the operation. Possible values are Add Update Delete and Other.

name: AzureActivity
description: Entries from the Azure Activity log that provides insight into any subscription-level or management group level events that have occurred in Azure.

columns.name: OperationNameValue
columns.description: Identifier of the operation e.g. Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/listAccountSas/action.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Level of the event. One of the following values: Critical, Error, Warning, Informational and Verbose.

columns.name: ActivityStatusValue
columns.description: Status of the operation in display-friendly format. Common values include Started, In Progress, Succeeded, Failed, Active, Resolved.

columns.name: ActivitySubstatusValue
columns.description: Substatus of the operation  in display-friendly format. E.g. OK (HTTP Status Code: 200).

columns.name: ResourceGroup
columns.description: Resource group name of the impacted resource.

columns.name: SubscriptionId
columns.description: Subscription ID of the impacted resource.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: Usually a GUID in the string format. Events that share a correlationId belong to the same uber action.

columns.name: Caller
columns.description: GUID of the caller.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: IP address of the user who has performed the operation UPN claim or SPN claim based on availability.

columns.name: CategoryValue
columns.description: Category of the activity log e.g. Administrative, Policy, Security.

columns.name: HTTPRequest
columns.description: Blob describing the Http Request. Usually includes the “clientRequestId”, “clientIpAddress” and “method” (HTTP method. For example, PUT).

columns.name: Properties
columns.description: Set of <Key Value> pairs (i.e. Dictionary) describing the details of the event. Stored as string. Usage of Properties_d is recommended instead.

columns.name: EventSubmissionTimestamp
columns.description: Timestamp when the event became available for querying.

columns.name: Authorization
columns.description: Blob of RBAC properties of the event. Usually includes the “action”, “role” and “scope” properties. Stored as string. The use of Authorization_d should be preferred going forward.

columns.name: OperationId
columns.description: GUID of the operation

columns.name: ResourceProviderValue
columns.description: Id of the resource provider for the impacted resource - e.g. Microsoft.Storage.

columns.name: EventDataId
columns.description: Unique identifier of an event.

columns.name: TenantId
columns.description: ID of the worksapce that stores this record

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp when the event was generated by the Azure service processing the request corresponding the event.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: Azure is used always for AzureActivity

columns.name: Authorization_d
columns.description: Blob of RBAC properties of the event. Usually includes the “action”, “role” and “scope” properties. Stored as dynamic column.

columns.name: Claims
columns.description: The JWT token used by Active Directory to authenticate the user or application to perform this operation in Resource Manager. The use of claims_d should be preferred going forward.

columns.name: Claims_d
columns.description: The JWT token used by Active Directory to authenticate the user or application to perform this operation in Resource Manager.

columns.name: Properties_d
columns.description: Set of <Key Value> pairs (i.e. Dictionary) describing the details of the event. Stored as dynamic column.

columns.name: Hierarchy
columns.description: Management group hierarchy of the management group or subscription that event belongs to.

columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

description: This table contains details of Azure Backup operations.

columns.name: TimeGenerated

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Category of the log, for example, AzureBackupOperations.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: High-level name of the action that is logged to this table, for example, DataOperations.

columns.name: OperationType
columns.description: Type of the Azure Backup operation executed, for example, stop backup with delete data, modify policy, change passphrase.

columns.name: OperationStartTime
columns.description: The start time of the operation.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: The schema version.

columns.name: ExtendedProperties

columns.name: BackupManagementType

name: AzureDevOpsAuditing
description: Schema for Azure DevOps audit logs, which can be used to track the many changes that occur within your Azure DevOps organization(s). Some examples include changes to security policies, pipelines, billing, and projects. For a full list of events, see aka.ms/azdev-audit-events.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: The identifier for the audit event, unique across services.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: CorrelationId allows two or more auditing events to be grouped together. This happens when a single action causes a cascade of auditing entries. An example being project creation.

columns.name: ActivityId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the action that occurred.

columns.name: ActorClientId
columns.description: When the action was performed by a managed identity or other service principal, this value represents the client ID of that principal. Otherwise, this value is 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000. When this field is populated, ActorCUID and ActorUserId will both be 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.

columns.name: ActorCUID
columns.description: When the action was performed by a user, this value represents a consistently unique identifier for that actor. Otherwise, this value is 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000. When this field, along with ActorUserId, is populated, ActorClientId will be 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.

columns.name: ActorUserId
columns.description: When the action was performed by a user or Azure DevOps service, this value represents that actor's user identifier. Otherwise, this value is 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000. When this field, along with ActorUserId, is populated, ActorClientId will be 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.

columns.name: ActorUPN
columns.description: The actor's user principal name.

columns.name: AuthenticationMechanism
columns.description: Type of authentication used by the actor.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time the auditing event occurred in UTC.

columns.name: ScopeType
columns.description: The level (scope) that the event occurred.

columns.name: ScopeDisplayName
columns.description: User friendly name for the scope level that an auditing event occurred at.

columns.name: ScopeId
columns.description: The organization identifier.

columns.name: ProjectId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the project that an auditing event occurred in. If not provided then the event isn't scoped to a particular project.

columns.name: ProjectName
columns.description: Friendly name of the project that an auditing event occurred in. If not provided then the event isn't scoped to a particular project.

columns.name: IpAddress
columns.description: IP address where the event originated.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The user agent from the request.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The unique identifier for the type of auditing event that occurred. For example, Git.CreateRepo identifies the an auditing event for Git repository creation.

columns.name: Data
columns.description: Additional data that's unique to the type of auditing event.

columns.name: Details
columns.description: Description of what happened.

columns.name: Area
columns.description: Part of the Azure DevOps product where the auditing event occurred.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Type of action that occurred when the auditing event was logged.

columns.name: CategoryDisplayName
columns.description: Type of action that occurred when the auditing event was logged.

columns.name: ActorDisplayName
columns.description: Display name of the user who initiated the auditing event to be logged.

columns.description: The name of the table

name: BehaviorEntities
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoints (MDE) behaviors table. Contains information about entities (file, process, device, user, and others) that are involved in a behavior or observation, including detected threats.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time when the record was generated.

columns.name: BehaviorId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the behavior.

columns.name: ActionType
columns.description: Type of activity that triggered the event. Associated with specific MITRE ATT&CK techniques.

columns.name: Categories
columns.description: Types of threat indicator or breach activity identified by the alert. Defined by the MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise.

columns.name: ServiceSource
columns.description: Product or service that provided the alert information.

columns.name: DetectionSource
columns.description: Detection technology or sensor that identified the notable component or activity.

columns.name: DataSources
columns.description: Products or services that provided information for the behavior.

columns.name: EntityType
columns.description: Type of object, such as a file, a process, a device, or a user.

columns.name: EntityRole
columns.description: Indicates whether the entity is impacted or merely related.

columns.name: DetailedEntityRole
columns.description: The role of the entity in the behavior

columns.name: FileName
columns.description: Name of the file involved in the alert. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: FolderPath
columns.description: Folder containing the file. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: SHA1
columns.description: SHA-1 hash of the file. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: SHA256
columns.description: SHA-256 of the file. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: FileSize
columns.description: Size of the file in bytes. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process"

columns.name: ThreatFamily
columns.description: Malware family that the suspicious or malicious file or process has been classified under.

columns.name: RemoteIP
columns.description: IP address that was being connected to.

columns.name: RemoteUrl
columns.description: URL or fully qualified domain name (FQDN) that was being connected to.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: User name of the account.

columns.name: AccountDomain
columns.description: Domain of the account.

columns.name: AccountSid
columns.description: Security Identifier (SID) of the account.

columns.name: AccountObjectId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the account in Azure AD.

columns.name: AccountUpn
columns.description: User principal name (UPN) of the account.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the device in the service.

columns.name: DeviceName
columns.description: Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the device.

columns.name: LocalIP
columns.description: IP address assigned to the local machine used during communication.

columns.name: NetworkMessageId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the email in UUID format, generated by Office 365.

columns.name: EmailSubject
columns.description: Subject of the email.

columns.name: EmailClusterId
columns.description: Identifier for the group of similar emails clustered based on heuristic analysis of their contents.

columns.name: Application
columns.description: Application that performed the recorded action.

columns.name: ApplicationId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the application.

columns.name: OAuthApplicationId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the third-party OAuth application in UUID format.

columns.name: ProcessCommandLine
columns.description: Command line used to create the new process.

columns.name: RegistryKey
columns.description: Registry key that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: RegistryValueName
columns.description: Name of the registry value that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: RegistryValueData
columns.description: Data of the registry value that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: AdditionalFields
columns.description: Additional information about the entity or event.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: CDBCassandraRequests
description: This table details data plane operations, specifically for Cassandra API accounts.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) of the Cassandra API data plane request.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB account against which this request was issued.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The Cassandra API operation that was executed.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The user agent suffix of the client issuing the request.

columns.name: ActivityId
columns.description: The unique identifier (GUID) for this Cassandra API request.

columns.name: ErrorCode
columns.description: The error code (if applicable) for this request.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: The server side execution time (in ms) for this request.

columns.name: RequestCharge
columns.description: The RU (Request Unit) consumption for this request.

columns.name: DatabaseName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB database against which this request was issued.

columns.name: CollectionName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB table/container against which this request was issued.

columns.name: AuthorizationTokenType
columns.description: The authorization token used for this request.

columns.name: RetryCount
columns.description: The number of server side retries issued for this request.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The IP address of the client that issued this request.

columns.name: PIICommandText
columns.description: Full query text with parameters (if opted in) for this request.

columns.name: RateLimitingDelayMs
columns.description: The estimated time (in ms) spent in retrying due to rate limited operations.

columns.name: RetriedDueToRateLimiting
columns.description: Boolean flag indicating if this request was retried server side due to throttles.

columns.name: RegionName
columns.description: The region against which this request was issued.

columns.name: RequestLength
columns.description: The payload size (in bytes) of the request.

columns.name: ResponseLength
columns.description: The payload size (in bytes) of the server response.

name: CDBControlPlaneRequests
description: This table details all control plane operations executed on the account, which include modifications to the regional failover policy, indexing policy, IAM role assignments, backup/restore policies, VNet and firewall rules, private links as well as updates and deletes of the account.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) when this Control Plane operation was executed against the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: ActivityId
columns.description: The Activity ID of the operation.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The Control Plane Operation that was executed.

columns.name: HttpStatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP status code of the control plane operation.

columns.name: Result
columns.description: The result of the operation indicating either success or failure.

columns.name: RegionName
columns.description: The region in which this control plan operation was executed.

columns.name: HttpMethod
columns.description: The HTTP method issued for this control plane operation.

columns.name: CurrentWriteRegion
columns.description: The current write region for this account (applies when a regional failover is triggered to choose a new write region).

columns.name: NewWriteRegion
columns.description: The new write region for the Cosmos DB account (after a user-initiated failover operation is executed).

columns.name: IpRangeFilter
columns.description: The IP range filter specified as part of the VNet rules for the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: EnableVirtualNetworkFilter
columns.description: Boolean flag indicating if VNet filters were enabled for the account.

columns.name: VirtualNetworkResourceEntries
columns.description: The list of IP addresses being included as part of the VNet rule for the account.

columns.name: EnablePrivateEndpointConnection
columns.description: Boolean flag to enable private endpoints for the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: PrivateEndpointConnections
columns.description: The list of private endpoints for the Cosmos DB account (in each region).

columns.name: PrivateEndpointArmUrl
columns.description: The ARM URL of the private endpoint created for the account.

columns.name: DefaultConsistencyLevel
columns.description: The default consistency level for the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: EnableAutomaticFailover
columns.description: Boolean flag to enable automatic failover for the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: MaxStalenessIntervalInSeconds
columns.description: The maximum staleness value (in seconds) for the Cosmos DB account when using the Bounded Staleness consistency setting.

columns.name: MaxStalenessPrefix
columns.description: The max staleness prefix for the Cosmos DB account when using the Bounded Staleness consistency setting.

columns.name: MultipleWriteLocations
columns.description: Boolean flag indicating if the Cosmos DB account is a multi-master account.

columns.name: Cors
columns.description: Collection of account’s Cross Origin Resource Sharing Rules

columns.name: BackupIntervalInMinutes
columns.description: The time (in minutes) between consecutive backup snapshots for the Cosmos DB account. Valid range: 60 - 1440 minutes.

columns.name: BackupRetentionIntervalInHours
columns.description: The duration of time (in hours) for which backup snapshots are retained for the Cosmos DB account. Valid range: 8 - 720 hours.

columns.name: BackupStorageRedundancy
columns.description: The type of backup storage redundancy for the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: TargetBackupMode
columns.description: The backup mode after update. This can be either Continuous or Periodic.

columns.name: TargetContinuousTier
columns.description: The continuous tier after updating. This can be either Continuous7Days or Continuous30Days.

columns.name: VnetResourceGroupName
columns.description: The name of the resource group within which the VNet is created.

columns.name: VirtualNetworkName
columns.description: The name of the Vnet for the account.

columns.name: VnetDatabaseAccountEntries
columns.description: The list of virtual networks specified for the account.

columns.name: AcledSubnets
columns.description: The ACL’d subnets for the account.

columns.name: VnetLocation
columns.description: The Azure region in which the VNet for the account is location.

columns.name: VnetArmUrl

name: CDBDataPlaneRequests
description: The DataPlaneRequests table captures every data plane operation for the Cosmos DB account. Data Plane requests are operations executed to create, update, delete or retrieve data within the account.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) when the data plane request was issued.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB account.

columns.name: OperationName

columns.name: ConnectionMode
columns.description: The connection mode used by the client issuing the request – (Direct or Gateway mode).

columns.name: ActivityId
columns.description: The unique identifier (GUID) for this data plane operation

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The name of the user specified user agent suffix (as specified when initializing the Cosmos DB client) when running in Direct mode.

columns.name: RequestResourceType
columns.description: The Cosmos DB resource type within the account against which the data plane request was executed, can be one of Database, Collection, Document, Attachment, User, Permission, StoredProcedure, Trigger, UserDefinedFunction, Offer.

columns.name: RequestResourceId
columns.description: The ID of the specific Cosmos DB resource within the account against which the data plane request was executed.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP status code response for the data plane request, highlighting details of the success/failure of the request.

columns.name: ClientIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the client VM issuing the request.

columns.name: RequestCharge
columns.description: The RUs (Request Units) consumed by this operation.

columns.name: DurationMs

columns.description: The payload size (in bytes) for the request.

columns.name: ResponseLength

columns.name: PartitionId
columns.description: The physical partition ID for the Cosmos DB container against which the request was issued.

columns.name: AadAppliedRoleAssignmentId
columns.description: The ID of the applied role assignment for the caller issuing the data plane request.

columns.name: AadPrincipalId

columns.name: DatabaseName

columns.name: CollectionName

columns.name: ResourceTokenPermissionId

columns.name: ResourceTokenPermissionMode
columns.description: The permission mode of the resource token associated with the resource accessed by this request.

columns.name: ResourceTokenUserRid
columns.description: The user ID associated with the resource token for the resource accessed by this request.

name: CDBDataPlaneRequests15M

name: CDBPartitionKeyStatistics
description: This table provides outlier logical partition keys that have consumed more storage space than others. Statistics are based on a sub-sampling of partition keys within the collection and hence these are approximate. Partition keys that are below 1GB of storage may not show up in the reported statistics.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) when statistics for this logical partition key were generated.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB account containing the dataset for which partition key stats were generated.

columns.name: RegionName
columns.description: The Azure region from which statistics for this partition were retrieved.

columns.name: PartitionKey
columns.description: The logical partition key for which storage statistics were retrieved.

columns.name: SizeKb
columns.description: The storage size (in KB) for the logical partition key within the physical partition.

columns.name: DatabaseName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB database, which contains the partition.

columns.name: CollectionName
columns.description: The name of the Cosmos DB collection, which contains the partition.
