name: CIEventsAudit
description: All API requests in the context of the Customer Insights instance, for example all user actions while configuring and using the instance. POST|PUT|DELETE|PATCH operations go into this category.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the log was generated.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Always ApiEvent, marking the log event as API event.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation represented by this event.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID for the correlated events. Can be used to identify correlated events between multiple tables.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Log category of the event. Either Operational or Audit. All POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE HTTP Requests are tagged with Audit, everything else with Operational.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Status of the event. Running, Skipped, Successful, Failure.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: Sub status of the event. If the operation corresponds to a REST API call, it's the HTTP status code.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Duration of the operation in milliseconds.

columns.name: CallerIPAddress
columns.description: Caller IP address, if the operation corresponds to an API call that would come from an entity with a publicly available IP address.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: Browser agent sending the request or unknown.

columns.name: Method
columns.description: HTTP method: GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/HEAD

columns.name: Path
columns.description: Relative path of the request.

columns.name: Origin
columns.description: URI indicating where a fetch originates from or unknown.

columns.name: OperationStatus
columns.description: Success for HTTP status code < 400, ClientError for HTTP status code < 500, Error for HTTP Status >= 500.

columns.name: CallerObjectId
columns.description: Azure Active Directory ObjectId of the caller.

columns.name: InstanceId
columns.description: Customer Insights instanceId.

columns.name: UserRole
columns.description: Assigned role for the user or app.

columns.name: RequiredRoles
columns.description: Required roles to do the operation. Admin role is allowed to do all operations.

columns.name: Claims
columns.description: Claims of the user or app JSON web token (JWT). Claim properties vary per organization and the Azure Active Directory configuration.

columns.name: UserPrincipalName
columns.description: The UserPrincipalName is the Azure AD username for the user accounts.

columns.name: Audience
columns.description: The audience for which the accessToken was requested for.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Severity level of the event, is one of: Informational, Warning, Error, or Critical.

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Absolute request URI.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

name: CIEventsOperational
description: Events generated using the service, for example GET requests or the execution events of a workflow.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp of the event (UTC).

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: The type of the event. Either ApiEvent or WorkflowEvent

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation represented by this event. {OperationType}.[WorkFlow|Task][Started|Completed].

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID for the correlated events. Can be used to identify correlated events between multiple tables.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Status of the event. Running, Skipped, Successful, Failure.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Duration of the operation in milliseconds.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Severity level of the event, is one of: Informational, Warning or Error.

columns.name: WorkflowJobId
columns.description: Identifier of the workflow run. All Workflow and Tasks events within the workflow execution have the same workflowJobId.

columns.name: OperationType
columns.description: Identifier of the operation.

columns.name: OperationStatus
columns.description: Success for HTTP Status code < 400, ClientError for HTTP Status code < 500, Error for HTTP Status >= 500.

columns.name: TasksCount
columns.description: Workflow only. Number of tasks the Workflow triggers.

columns.name: SubmittedBy
columns.description: Workflow events only. The Azure Active Directory objectId of the user who triggered the workflow, see also properties.workflowSubmissionKinds.

columns.name: WorkflowType
columns.description: Full or incremental refresh.

columns.name: WorkflowSubmissionKind
columns.description: OnDemand or Scheduled.

columns.name: WorkflowStatus
columns.description: Running, Successful.

columns.name: StartTime
columns.description: Specifies the date and time that the workflow job was started (UTC)

columns.name: EndTime
columns.description: Specifies the date and time that the workflow job ended (UTC)

columns.name: SubmittedTime
columns.description: Specifies the date and time that the workflow job was submitted (UTC)

columns.name: InstanceId
columns.description: Customer Insights instance ID.

columns.name: Identifier
columns.description: Depending on the OperationType in can be: the guid of the export configuration, the guid of the Enrichment, the Entity Name.

columns.name: FriendlyName
columns.description: User friendly Name of the Export or the Entity which is processed.

columns.name: Error
columns.description: Error Message with more details.

columns.name: AdditionalInformation
columns.description: Contains AffectedEntities, MessageCode and entityCount.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Log category of the event. Either Operational or Audit. All POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE HTTP Requests are tagged with Audit, everything else with Operational.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: Sub status of the event. If the operation corresponds to a REST API call, it's the HTTP status code.

columns.name: CallerIPAddress
columns.description: Caller IP address, if the operation corresponds to an API call that would come from an entity with a publicly available IP address.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: Browser agent sending the request or unknown.

columns.name: Method
columns.description: HTTP method: GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/HEAD

columns.name: Path
columns.description: Relative path of the request.

columns.name: Origin
columns.description: URI indicating where a fetch originates from or unknown.

columns.name: CallerObjectId
columns.description: Azure Active Directory ObjectId of the caller.

columns.name: UserRole
columns.description: Assigned role for the user or app.

columns.name: RequiredRoles
columns.description: Required roles to do the operation. Admin role is allowed to do all operations.

columns.name: Claims
columns.description: Claims of the user or app JSON web token (JWT). Claim properties vary per organization and the Azure Active Directory configuration.

columns.name: UserPrincipalName
columns.description: The UserPrincipalName is the Azure AD username for the user accounts.

columns.name: Audience
columns.description: The audience for which the accessToken was requested for.

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Absolute request URI.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: User

columns.name: BatchId

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Value is either ATTEMPT or FAILED.

columns.name: Operation
columns.description: The CQL statement or a textual description of the operation.

columns.name: OperationNaked
columns.description: The CQL statement or a textual description of the operation, without bound values appended to prepared statements.

columns.name: ExternalUserId

name: CassandraLogs
description: Cassandra general logging messages (system.log).

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the record was created.

columns.name: ClusterName
columns.description: Cluster name.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Severity level of the event, will be one of INFO, WARM, ERROR, or OFF.

columns.name: AddressIp
columns.description: IP address of the node that generated the logging event.

columns.name: ThreadName
columns.description: Name of the thread that generated the logging event.

columns.name: ThreadId
columns.description: ID of the thread that generated the logging event.

columns.name: SourceFile
columns.description: File name where the logging request was issued.

columns.name: SourceLine
columns.description: Line number where the logging request was issued.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: Application supplied message associated with the logging event.

columns.name: Exception
columns.description: Throwable trace bound to the logging event, by default this will output the full trace as one would normally find with a call to Throwable.printStackTrace().

columns.name: EventProduct
columns.description: Product of the logging event, e.g. cassandra, dse, solr.

columns.name: EventCategory
columns.description: Category of the logging event, e.g. startup, garbage_collection, compaction.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: type of the logging event, e.g. commit_log_replay_skipped, unknown_table.

columns.name: CassandraKeyspace
columns.description: Cassandra keyspace.

columns.name: CassandraTable
columns.description: Cassandra table.

columns.name: PartitionKey
columns.description: Partition key.

columns.name: PartitionSize
columns.description: Partition size.

columns.name: TombstonedCells
columns.description: The number of tombstoned cells.

columns.name: LiveCells
columns.description: The number of live cells.

columns.name: CassandraKey
columns.description: Cassandra key.

columns.name: RequestedColumns
columns.description: The number of columns requested.

columns.name: SliceStart
columns.description: The start of the column slice inclusive.

columns.name: SliceEnd
columns.description: The end of the the column slice inclusive.

columns.name: DeletionInfo
columns.description: Deletion info.

columns.name: CodeCacheBefore
columns.description: Code cache before garbage collection (in bytes). Code cache stores native code generated by JVM.

columns.name: CodeCacheAfter
columns.description: Code cache after garbage collection (in bytes). Code cache stores native code generated by JVM.

columns.name: Collections
columns.description: The number of garbage collections.

columns.name: CompressedClassSpaceAfter
columns.description: Compressed class space after garbage collection (in bytes). Compressed class space stores class information referred to by the objects in the JavaHeap.

columns.name: CompressedClassSpaceBefore
columns.description: Compressed class space before garbage collection (in bytes). Compressed class space stores class information referred to by the objects in the JavaHeap.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Duration.

columns.name: EdenSpaceBefore
columns.description: Eden space before garbage collection (in bytes). Eden space is the memory region where objects are allocated when they are created.

columns.name: EdenSpaceAfter
columns.description: Eden space after garbage collection (in bytes). Eden space is the memory region where objects are allocated when they are created.

columns.name: GCType
columns.description: The type of garbage collection, e.g. ParNew, MarkSweepCompact, G1 Old.

columns.name: MaxMemory
columns.description: The maximum amount of memory (in bytes) that can be used for memory management.

columns.name: MetaspaceBefore
columns.description: Metaspace before garbage collection (in bytes). Metaspace stores classes metadata.

columns.name: MetaspaceAfter
columns.description: Metaspace after garbage collection (in bytes). Metaspace stores classes metadata.

columns.name: OldGenAfter
columns.description: Old Generation after garbage collection (in bytes). Old Generation is used to store long surviving objects.

columns.name: OldGenBefore
columns.description: Old Generation before garbage collection (in bytes). Old Generation is used to store long surviving objects.

columns.name: PercentFull
columns.description: The percentage of full heap.

columns.name: PermGenAfter
columns.description: Permanent Generation space after garbage collection (in bytes). Permanent generation stores classes metadata (renamed to Metaspace in Java 8).

columns.name: PermGenBefore
columns.description: Permanent Generation space before garbage collection (in bytes). Permanent generation stores classes metadata (renamed to Metaspace in Java 8).

columns.name: SurvivorSpaceBefore
columns.description: Survivor space before garbage collection (in bytes). Survivor space stores the objects that have survived the garbage collection of the Eden space.

columns.name: SurvivorSpaceAfter
columns.description: Survivor space after garbage collection (in bytes). Survivor space stores the objects that have survived the garbage collection of the Eden space.

columns.name: UsedMemory
columns.description: The amount of memory currently used (in bytes).

columns.name: DroppedCrossNodeTimeout
columns.description: The number of messages dropped due to cross node timeout in last 5000ms.

columns.name: DroppedInternalTimeout
columns.description: The number of messages dropped due to internal timeout in last 5000ms.

columns.name: DroppedMessagesType
columns.description: The type of messages dropped in last 5000ms.

columns.name: DroppedMessages
columns.description: The number of messages dropped in last 5000ms.

columns.name: Endpoint
columns.description: IP address of an endpoint.

columns.name: HostId
columns.description: The GUID assigned in the Cassandra cluster to uniquely identify this node. See the system.local table or nodetool status to find information about the host by its HostId.

columns.name: PendingTasks
columns.description: The number of pending tasks in gossip stage.

columns.name: SessionId
columns.description: Unique string identifying what query was running when this log was emitted. Use SHOW SESSION <SessionId> to find details of the query's activity.

columns.name: SSTableName
columns.description: SSTable name.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

name: CloudHsmServiceOperationAuditLogs
description: This table contains HSM Commands send to your Azure Cloud HSM resource's HSM partitions. These logs capture all HSM operations performed by Customer over E2E channel on each HSM partition of that Cloud HSM resource. They can be used to monitor events and configure necessary alerts on your Cloud HSM resource.

columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when operation occured.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Result of the request.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: IP address of the client that made the request.

columns.name: ClientSdkPackageVersion
columns.description: Version of the client SDK package.

columns.name: MemberId
columns.description: Member ID of HSM in the Cloud HSM cluster.

columns.name: Opcode
columns.description: Operation code in HEX string format.

columns.name: ClientInfo
columns.description: User agent information.

columns.name: Sku
columns.description: Information about the Cloud HSM SKU including family and name.

columns.name: PoolType
columns.description: Cloud HSM pool type.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: Short signature of the result.

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: More detailed description of the result.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Time it took to service the request, in milliseconds. This does not include the network latency, so the time you measure on the client side might not match this time.

name: ContainerLogV2
description: Kubernetes Container logs in V2 schema. This is the successor of ContainerLog. This has a friendlier schema, specifically for Kubernetes orchestrated containers in pods. With this feature enabled, previously split container logs are stitched together and sent as single entries to the ContainerLogV2 table. The schema now supports container log lines of up to to 64 KB. The schema also supports .NET and Go stack traces, which appear as single entries.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Name of the Computer/Node generating the log.

columns.name: ContainerId
columns.description: Container ID of the log source as seen by the Container engine.

columns.name: ContainerName
columns.description: Name of the Container generating the log.

columns.name: PodName
columns.description: Kubernetes Pod name for the Container generating the log.

columns.name: PodNamespace
columns.description: Kubernetes Namespace for the container's pod.

columns.name: LogMessage
columns.description: Log message from stdout or stderr. Being a dynamic field, json log messages can be queried without parse_json.

columns.name: LogSource
columns.description: Source of the Log message. Possible vlaues are stdout or stderr.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the log was generated.

columns.name: KubernetesMetadata
columns.description: Kubernetes Metadata including podUid, podLabels, podAnnotations and container image details, etc.

columns.name: LogLevel
columns.description: Categorize logs based on importance and severity. Possible values: CRITICAL, ERROR, WARNING, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, UNKNOWN.

columns.name: _ResourceId

columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: DestinationIdentity
columns.description: Security identity number for the destination (flow.destination.identity).

columns.name: DestinationClusterName
columns.description: The name of the destination cluster (flow.destination.cluster_name).

columns.name: DestinationNamespace
columns.description: The namespace of the destination (flow.destination.namespace).

columns.name: DestinationPodName
columns.description: The name of the destination pod (flow.destination.pod_name).

columns.name: DestinationWorkloads
columns.description: Array of workloads associated with the destination, including name and kind (flow.destination.workloads).

columns.name: FlowType
columns.description: Type of the flow (e.g., L3_L4, L7 SOCK) (flow.Type).

columns.name: NodeName
columns.description: Name of the node where the flow was captured (flow.node_name).

columns.name: Layer7
columns.description: L7 flow type if Flow_Type is L7 (e.g., DNS, HTTP, Kafka) (flow.l7.type).

columns.name: Reply
columns.description: Indicates if the flow is a reply (flow.is_reply.value).

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Event type details (flow.event_type).

columns.name: Service
columns.description: Service details of the flow.

columns.name: TrafficDirection
columns.description: Direction of the traffic (e.g., INGRESS, EGRESS) (flow.traffic_direction).

columns.name: TraceObservationPoint
columns.description: Point of observation in the trace (e.g., TO_ENDPOINT) (flow.trace_observation_point).

columns.name: IngressFlowCount
columns.description: Number of packets sent from the source to the destination since the last update.

columns.name: EgressFlowCount
columns.description: Number of packets sent from the destination to the source since the last update.

columns.name: UnknownDirectionFlowCount
columns.description: Number of unknown flows since the last update.

columns.name: Policies
columns.description: Combined entry for all policies that allowed or denied ingress/egress (flow.egress_allowed_by, flow.ingress_allowed_by, flow.egress_denied_by, flow.ingress_denied_by).

columns.name: AdditionalFlowData
columns.description: Additional flow data.

name: DNSQueryLogs
description: DNS query logs enable customers to monitor the DNS traffic in their virtual networks and help securing their DNS infrastructure.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Time (UTC) when the log was created.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: Version
columns.description: The version number of the query log format.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The region where the virtual network was created in.

columns.name: VirtualNetworkId
columns.description: The ID of the virtual network that the query originated in.

columns.name: QueryName
columns.description: The domain name (contoso.com) or subdomain name (www.contoso.com) that was specified in the query.

columns.name: QueryType
columns.description: Either the DNS record type that was specified in the request, or ANY.

columns.name: QueryClass
columns.description: Specifies the protocol family. For example, IN for Internet.

columns.name: ResponseCode
columns.description: Response code that resolver returned in response to the DNS query.

columns.name: Answer
columns.description: Array of answers for DNS query.

columns.name: Authority
columns.description: Array of authority DNS servers for DNS query.

columns.name: AdditionalRecords
columns.description: Array of additional resource records.

columns.name: SourceIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the instance that the query originated from.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: The port on the instance that the query originated from.

columns.name: DestinationIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the instance that the query was sent to (outbound endpoints).

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: The port on the instance that the query was sent to.

columns.name: Transport
columns.description: The protocol (UDP or TCP) used to submit the DNS query.

columns.name: PrivateResolverEndpointId
columns.description: The ID of the resolver endpoint. Can be inbound, or outbound.

columns.name: QueryResponseTime
columns.description: Response time for resolution of DNS query.

columns.name: ResolutionPath
columns.description: Resolution path can be private zones, ruleset, or public DNS resolution.

columns.name: ResolverPolicyId
columns.description: The ID of the security policy which filtered the query.

columns.name: ResolverPolicyRuleAction
columns.description: Result after evaluation of the policy rules.

columns.name: ResolverPolicyDomainListId
columns.description: The ID of the domain list which was hit.

columns.name: DnsForwardingRulesetId
columns.description: The ID of the DNS forwarding ruleset which was hit.

columns.name: DnsForwardingRulesetDomain
columns.description: The domain which was hit in the DNS Forwarding ruleset.

name: DSMAzureBlobStorageLogs
description: Azure Blob Storage resource logs enriched with data sensitivity context provided by Azure Purview.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time (UTC) when the first request was received by storage.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The location of storage account.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The type of REST operation that was performed. For example: GetBlob, DeleteBlob.

columns.name: AuthenticationType
columns.description: The type of authentication that was used to make the request. E.g. OAuth, SAS, etc.

columns.name: RequesterAppId
columns.description: The Open Authorization (OAuth) application ID that is used as the requester.

columns.name: RequesterUpn
columns.description: The user principal names (UPN) of requestor.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP status code for the request. If the request is interrupted, this value might be set to Unknown.

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Uniform resource identifier that is requested.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the requester.

columns.name: UserAgentHeader
columns.description: The user-agent header value.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: The category of requested operation.

columns.name: AggregationCount
columns.description: Number of events that were aggregated into a single entry.

columns.name: AggregationLastEventTime
columns.description: The time (UTC) when the last request was received by storage.

columns.name: SumResponseBodySize
columns.description: The sum of packets in responses written by the storage service, in bytes. If request(s) are unsuccessful, this value may be empty.

columns.name: ResourceSubscriptionId
columns.description: The subscription ID (GUID) of the storage account being accessed.

columns.name: ResourceGroup
columns.description: The Resource Group name of the storage account that was accessed.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: The name of the storage account.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID that is used to correlate resource logs with data sensitivity logs.

columns.description: The name of the table

name: DSMDataClassificationLogs
description: Contains data classification information provided by Azure Purview and is used to correlate storage resource logs with data sensitivity information.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time (UTC) when Azure Purview scan of asset occurred.

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Uniform resource identifier representing the asset that was scanned.

columns.name: Classifications
columns.description: JSON containing the list of classifications that were discovered.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID that is used to correlate resource logs with data sensitivity logs.

columns.name: SourceType
columns.description: Type of resource that was scanned by Azure Purview (Azure Blob, Azure File, etc.).

columns.name: AssetType
columns.description: Type of asset that was scanned by Azure Purview (e.g., File, Table).

columns.name: AssetLastScanTime
columns.description: The time (UTC) when the resource scan for sensitivity was performed by Azure Purview.

columns.name: ClassificationDetails
columns.description: For every classification found in the resource - corresponding Instance Count (i.e. how many occurrences of a specific type of classification was present) and Confidence (i.e. Match Accuracy) is listed.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Uniform resource identifier representing the resource that was scanned.

columns.name: SensitivityLabelName
columns.description: The name of sensitive label found and/or applied.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID that is used to correlate resource logs with data sensitivity logs.

columns.name: AssetLastScanTime
columns.description: The time (UTC) when the resource scan for sensitivity was performed by Azure Purview.

name: DataSetOutput
description: The raw collected data from DCR datasets.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time (UTC) when the log was generated. This column can be used to construct a time series or to filter data to a specific time window. Example: 2014-05-25T08:20:03.123456Z.

columns.name: DataSetRunId
columns.description: The identifier of the Data Set run for which this data was collected.

columns.name: RawData
columns.description: An arbitrary string containing the collected data for this log record. The data format, encoding, etc. will vary by collection. Local transformation of collected data is supported. Example: 'svchost.exe','1996','0','Unknown','8,384K', '0:00:06'.

columns.name: RawDataLabel
columns.description: Short string that uniquely identifies the format of the collected RawData. Example: Microsoft.TaskList-csv.

columns.name: DataSourceName
columns.description: Data source name as provided by DCR. Example: GenerateTaskList.

columns.name: DataSetRunSeqNum
columns.description: A monotonically increasing counter for each log collected during a data set collection.Enables sequential ordering of logs that may have identical timestamps and identifying lost logs within a collection instance.

name: DataSetRuns
description: This table contains status and other information about data sources that were collected as part of DCR datasets.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time (UTC) when the log was generated. This column can be used to construct a time series or to filter data to a specific time window. Example: 2014-05-25T08:20:03.123456Z.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: The name field from the DCR used for data collection. Example: CollectPeformanceReport.

columns.name: DataSetRunId
columns.description: Randomly generated unique indentifier (brace-less UUID) for each collecction instance. This column enables selecting all data associated with the same collection.

columns.name: DCRId
columns.description: The ARM resource Id of the Data Collection Rule (DCR) that produced the data. This column enables selecting all data associated with the same DCR.

columns.name: CorrelationParams
columns.description: A set of parameters, in JSON, that provide identifiers or other strings used to correlate triggers or workflows that initiated the data set run. For example, if the collection was triggered by an Alert, this column will contain the Alert Resource ID. This column may be empty if the data set run has no known correlations. This column enables selecting all data associated with a common trigger.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The operation status of the data set collection. This status could apply to the data set or data source. Example: DataSetRunInProgress or DataSourceCollectionSucceeded.

columns.name: StatusDetail
columns.description: Additional information about the status of the data set collection, in JSON.

columns.name: TimeoutMinutes
columns.description: The duration, in minutes, that a data set collection or data source collection can execute before timing out.

name: DataTransferOperations
description: Logs generated by Azure Data Transfer as objects are transferred. These logs can be used to determine if an object has successfully transferred, failed to transfer, or is in the process of transferring. A typical use case would be an objects latest status of 'InTransit' indicating that the object is still transferring and no action needs to be taken.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) when the log was created.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: Correlates different log messages for the same object.

columns.name: FlowId
columns.description: The internal ID of the customer's flow.

columns.name: ObjectName
columns.description: Name of the customer's object.

columns.name: ObjectLastUpdated
columns.description: Timestamp (in UTC) when the object was last updated.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The status of the object as it is transferred. The status will let a customer know when the transfer has started, when it has finished, and if it has failed.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Description of the object's state as it is transferred.

name: DeviceBehaviorEntities
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoints (MDE) behaviors table. Contains information about entities (file, process, device, user, and others) that are involved in a behavior or observation, including detected threats.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time when the record was generated.

columns.name: BehaviorId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the behavior.

columns.name: ActionType
columns.description: Type of activity that triggered the event. Associated with specific MITRE ATT&CK techniques.

columns.name: Categories
columns.description: Types of threat indicator or breach activity identified by the alert. Defined by the MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise.

columns.name: ServiceSource
columns.description: Product or service that provided the alert information.

columns.name: DetectionSource
columns.description: Detection technology or sensor that identified the notable component or activity.

columns.name: DataSources
columns.description: Products or services that provided information for the behavior.

columns.name: EntityType
columns.description: Type of object, such as a file, a process, a device, or a user.

columns.name: EntityRole
columns.description: Indicates whether the entity is impacted or merely related.

columns.name: DetailedEntityRole
columns.description: The role of the entity in the behavior

columns.name: FileName
columns.description: Name of the file involved in the alert. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: FolderPath
columns.description: Folder containing the file. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: SHA1
columns.description: SHA-1 hash of the file. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: SHA256
columns.description: SHA-256 of the file. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process".

columns.name: FileSize
columns.description: Size of the file in bytes. Empty unless EntityType is "File" or "Process"

columns.name: ThreatFamily
columns.description: Malware family that the suspicious or malicious file or process has been classified under.

columns.name: RemoteIP
columns.description: IP address that was being connected to.

columns.name: RemoteUrl
columns.description: URL or fully qualified domain name (FQDN) that was being connected to.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: User name of the account.

columns.name: AccountDomain
columns.description: Domain of the account.

columns.name: AccountSid
columns.description: Security Identifier (SID) of the account.

columns.name: AccountObjectId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the account in Azure AD.

columns.name: AccountUpn
columns.description: User principal name (UPN) of the account.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the device in the service.

columns.name: DeviceName
columns.description: Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the device.

columns.name: LocalIP
columns.description: IP address assigned to the local machine used during communication.

columns.name: NetworkMessageId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the email in UUID format, generated by Office 365.

columns.name: EmailSubject
columns.description: Subject of the email.

columns.name: EmailClusterId
columns.description: Identifier for the group of similar emails clustered based on heuristic analysis of their contents.

columns.name: Application
columns.description: Application that performed the recorded action.

columns.name: ApplicationId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the application.

columns.name: OAuthApplicationId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the third-party OAuth application in UUID format.

columns.name: ProcessCommandLine
columns.description: Command line used to create the new process.

columns.name: RegistryKey
columns.description: Registry key that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: RegistryValueName
columns.description: Name of the registry value that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: RegistryValueData
columns.description: Data of the registry value that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: AdditionalFields
columns.description: Additional information about the entity or event.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.description: User name of the account that ran the process responsible for the event.

columns.description: Azure AD object ID of the user account that ran the process responsible for the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessAccountUpn

columns.description: Command line used to run the process that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessFileName
columns.description: Name of the process that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessFolderPath
columns.description: Folder containing the process (image file) that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessId
columns.description: Process ID (PID) of the process that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel
columns.description: Integrity level of the process that initiated the event. Windows assigns integrity levels to processes based on certain characteristics, such as if they were launched from an internet download. These integrity levels influence permissions to resources.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessMD5
columns.description: MD5 hash of the process (image file) that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessParentFileName
columns.description: Name of the parent process that spawned the process responsible for the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessParentId
columns.description: Process ID (PID) of the parent process that spawned the process responsible for the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessSHA1
columns.description: SHA-1 hash of the process (image file) that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessSHA256
columns.description: SHA-256 hash of the process (image file) that initiated the event. This field is usually not populated - use the SHA1 column when available.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessTokenElevation
columns.description: Token type indicating the presence or absence of User Access Control (UAC) privilege elevation applied to the process that initiated the event.

columns.name: IsAzureInfoProtectionApplied
columns.description: Indicates whether the file is encrypted by Azure Information Protection.

columns.name: MD5
columns.description: MD5 hash of the file that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: MachineGroup
columns.description: Machine group of the machine. This group is used by role-based access control to determine access to the machine.

columns.name: PreviousFileName
columns.description: Original name of the file that was renamed as a result of the action.

columns.name: PreviousFolderPath
columns.description: Original folder containing the file before the recorded action was applied.

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: Event identifier based on a repeating counter. To identify unique events, this column must be used in conjunction with the ComputerName and EventTime columns.

columns.name: RequestAccountDomain
columns.description: Domain of the account used to remotely initiate the activity.

columns.name: RequestAccountName
columns.description: User name of account used to remotely initiate the activity.

columns.name: RequestAccountSid
columns.description: Security Identifier (SID) of the account used to remotely initiate the activity.

columns.name: RequestProtocol
columns.description: Network protocol, if applicable, used to initiate the activity: Unknown, Local, SMB, or NFS.

columns.name: RequestSourceIP
columns.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address of the remote device that initiated the activity.

columns.name: RequestSourcePort
columns.description: Source port on the remote device that initiated the activity.

columns.name: SHA1
columns.description: SHA-1 hash of the file that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: SHA256
columns.description: SHA-256 of the file that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: SensitivityLabel
columns.description: Label applied to an email, file, or other content to classify it for information protection.

columns.name: SensitivitySubLabel
columns.description: Sublabel applied to an email, file, or other content to classify it for information protection; sensitivity sublabels are grouped under sensitivity labels but are treated independently.

columns.name: ShareName
columns.description: Name of shared folder containing the file.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the event was recorded by the MDE agent on the endpoint.

columns.name: InitiatingProcessParentCreationTime

columns.name: InitiatingProcessCreationTime

columns.name: InitiatingProcessFileSize

name: EGNFailedHttpDataPlaneOperations
description: Log for failed HTTP data plane requests to an Event Grid namespace. It can be used for auditing purposes.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the log was generated.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The name of the operation.

columns.name: NetworkAccess
columns.description: The type of network used by the client issuing the request. Allowed values are: PublicAccess - when connecting via public IP, PrivateAccess - when connecting via private link

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the client issuing the request.

columns.name: TLSVersion
columns.description: The transport layer security (TLS) version used by the client connection. Possible values are: 1.2 and 1.3

columns.name: AuthenticationType
columns.description: Type of authentication used by the client. SharedAccessKey – request uses the SAS key, SharedAccessSignature – request uses a SAS token generated from SAS key, EntraIdAccessToken – Microsoft Entra issued JSON Web Token (JWT) token, Unknown – None of the above authentication types.

columns.name: ObjectId
columns.description: The Microsoft Entra ObjectId of the caller issuing the request.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: The type of the result. Possible values are: Failed.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: The result of the operation. Possible values are: ServiceError, ClientError, QuotaExceeded, AuthnError, AuthzError, ConnectionLost

columns.name: TotalOperations
columns.description: The total number of request with above values issued within the minute. These traces aren't emitted for each request. An aggregate for each unique combination of above values is emitted every minute

name: EnrichedMicrosoft365AuditLogs
description: This table is part of Identity and Network Access, which contains Enriched Microsoft 365 Audit logs. These logs can be leveraged for policy, risk, and traffic management, as well as to monitor users experience.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The date and time in UTC when the user performed the activity.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: Unique identifier of an audit record.

columns.name: RecordType
columns.description: The type of operation indicated by the record. See the AuditLogRecordType table for details on the types of audit log records.

columns.name: Operation
columns.description: The name of the user or admin activity that performed the activity.

columns.name: OrganizationId
columns.description: The GUID for your organization's Office 365 tenant. This value will always be the same for your organization, regardless of the Office 365 service in which it occurs.

columns.name: UserType
columns.description: The type of user that performed the operation.

columns.name: ActorUserType
columns.description: The type of user that performed the operation. Possible types includes: Admin, System, Application, Service Principal and Other.

columns.name: UserKey
columns.description: An alternative ID for the user identified in the UserId property. For example, this property is populated with the passport unique ID (PUID) for events performed by users in SharePoint, OneDrive for Business, and Exchange. This property may also specify the same value as the UserID property for events occurring in other services and events performed by system accounts.

columns.name: Workload
columns.description: The Office 365 service where the activity occurred.

columns.name: ResultStatus
columns.description: Indicates whether the action (specified in the Operation property) was successful or not. Possible values are Succeeded, PartiallySucceeded, or Failed.

columns.name: ObjectId
columns.description: For SharePoint and OneDrive for business activity, the full path name of the file or folder accessed by the user. For Exchange admin audit logging, the name of the object that was modified by the cmdlet.

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: The UPN (User Principal Name) of the user who performed the action (specified in the Operation property) that resulted in the record being logged; for example, my_name@my_domain_name. Note that records for activity performed by system accounts (such as SHAREPOINT\system or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM) are also included. In SharePoint, another value display in the UserId property is app@sharepoint. This indicates that the "user" who performed the activity was an application that has the necessary permissions in SharePoint to perform organization-wide actions (such as search a SharePoint site or OneDrive account) on behalf of a user, admin, or service. For more information, see the app@sharepoint user in audit records.

columns.name: ClientIp
columns.description: The IP address of the device that was used when the activity was logged. The IP address is displayed in either an IPv4 or IPv6 address format. For some services, the value displayed in this property might be the IP address for a trusted application (for example, Office on the web apps) calling into the service on behalf of a user and not the IP address of the device used by person who performed the activity. Also, for Azure Active Directory-related events, the IP address isn't logged and the value for the ClientIP property is null.

columns.name: UniqueTokenId
columns.description: The unique token identifier.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: The ID of the source device as reported in the record.

columns.name: DeviceOperatingSystem
columns.description: The client connecting operating system type.

columns.name: DeviceOperatingSystemVersion
columns.description: The client connecting operating system version.

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: The IP address from which the connection or session originated.

columns.name: AdditionalProperties
columns.description: Additional activity fields.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: FailedIngestion
description: Failed ingestion operations logs provide detailed information about failed ingest operations. Logs include data source details, as well as error code and failure status (transient or permanent), that can be used for tracking the process of data source ingestion. Users can identify usage errors (permanent bad requests) and handle retries of transient failures. Ingestion logs are supported for queued ingestion to the ingestion endpoint using SDKs, data connections, and connectors.

columns.name: FailedOn
columns.description: Time at which this ingest operation failed

columns.name: OperationId
columns.description: The ingestion's operation ID

columns.name: Database
columns.description: The name of the database holding the target table

columns.name: Table
columns.description: The name of the target table into which the data is ingested

columns.name: IngestionSourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier representing the ingested source

columns.name: IngestionSourcePath
columns.description: The path of the ingestion data sources or the Azure blob storage URI

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: The final state of this data ingestion operation

columns.name: RootActivityId
columns.description: The ingestion's activity ID

columns.name: Details
columns.description: Detailed description of the failure and error message

columns.name: ErrorCode
columns.description: The failure's error code

columns.name: FailureStatus
columns.description: The failure's status. `Permanent`, or `RetryAttemptsExceeded` indicates that the operation exceeded the max retries or max time limit following a recurring transient error

columns.name: OriginatesFromUpdatePolicy
columns.description: Indicates whether or not the failure originate from an update policy

columns.name: ShouldRetry
columns.description: Indicates whether or not the failure is transient and should be retried

name: HDInsightStormTopologyMetrics
description: Topology Level Metrics from Storm clusters.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the log was generated.

columns.name: Acked
columns.description: The number of Tuple “trees” successfully processed. A value of 0 is expected if no acking is done.

columns.name: AssignedCPUPercent
columns.description: Percent of CPU cores assigned to the topology.

columns.name: AssignedMemOffHeapMB
columns.description: MB of off heap memory assigned to the topology.

columns.name: AssignedMemOnHeapMB
columns.description: MB of on heap memory assigned to the topology.

columns.name: AssignedTotalMemMB
columns.description: MB of total memory assigned to the topology.

columns.name: BoltId
columns.description: The ID of the bolt.

columns.name: Capacity
columns.description: If this is around 1.0, the corresponding Bolt is running as fast as it can, so you may want to increase the Bolt’s parallelism. This is (number executed * average execute latency) / measurement time.

columns.name: ClusterName
columns.description: The name of the cluster.

columns.name: ClusterType
columns.description: The type of the cluster.

columns.name: CompleteLatencyMs
columns.description: The average time (millisecond) a Tuple “tree” takes to be completely processed by the Topology. A value of 0 is expected if no acking is done.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID for correlated events. Can be used to identify correlated events between multiple tables.

columns.name: Debug
columns.description: Boolean representing whether debug tracing is activated.

columns.name: Emitted
columns.description: The number of Tuples emitted.

columns.name: EncodedBoltId
columns.description: The encoded ID of the bolt.

columns.name: EncodedId
columns.description: The enocded ID of the topology.

columns.name: EncodedSpoutId
columns.description: The encoded ID of the Spout.

columns.name: ErrorHost
columns.description: Host where the error occurred.

columns.name: ErrorPort
columns.description: Port associated with the error.

columns.name: ErrorWorkerLogLink
columns.description: Link to the log of the worker where an error occurred.

columns.name: Executed
columns.description: The number of incoming Tuples processed.

columns.name: ExecuteLatencyMs
columns.description: The average time (millisecond) a Tuple spends in the execute method. The execute method may complete without sending an Ack for the tuple.

columns.name: Executors
columns.description: The number of threads being used to execute a task.

columns.name: ExecutorsTotal
columns.description: The total amount of executors currently used and already used to execute a task.

columns.name: Failed
columns.description: The number of Tuple “trees” that were explicitly failed or timed out before acking was completed. A value of 0 is expected if no acking is done.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: Hostname of the host the record came from.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: The name of the component the record is from (could be spout, bolt, or name of topology).

columns.name: InstanceName
columns.description: Type of record shape (there are bolt, spout, topology, and topology_stats record shapes).

columns.name: LastError
columns.description: Last error to occur in the component.

columns.name: MsgTimeout
columns.description: The number of seconds until a message times out.

columns.name: TopologyName
columns.description: Name of the topology.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The operation associated with log record.

columns.name: Owner
columns.description: Name of the user that owns the topology.

columns.name: ProcessLatencyMs
columns.description: The average time (millisecond) it takes to Ack a Tuple after it is first received. Bolts that join, aggregate or batch may not Ack a tuple until a number of other Tuples have been received(.

columns.name: ReplicationCount
columns.description: The amount of replicas kept by the topology.

columns.name: RequestedCpuPercent
columns.description: The percent of CPU requested by the topology.

columns.name: RequestMemOffHeapMB
columns.description: MB of off heap memory requested by the topology.

columns.name: RequestMemOnHeapMB
columns.description: MB of on heap memory requested by the topology.

columns.name: SamplingPct
columns.description: Percentage of messages sampled to calculate metrics.

columns.name: SchedulerDisplayResource
columns.description: Boolean describing the scheduler display setting.

columns.name: SpoutId
columns.description: The ID of the spout.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The status of the topology.

columns.name: Tasks
columns.description: The number of tasks running.

columns.name: TasksTotal
columns.description: The total amount of tasks run.

columns.name: TopologyId
columns.description: The ID of the topology.

columns.name: Transferred
columns.description: The number of Tuples emitted that sent to one or more bolts.

columns.name: Uptime
columns.description: The length of time an Executor (thread) has been alive.

columns.name: UptimeSeconds
columns.description: The amount of time the topology has been running in seconds.

columns.name: Window
columns.description: The time window for the metrics in the record in seconds.

columns.name: WindowHint
columns.description: The time window for the metrics in the record in hours, minutes, seconds format.

columns.name: WindowPretty
columns.description: String description of the time window for the metrics in the record.

columns.name: Workers
columns.description: JSON with worker specific metrics.

columns.name: WorkersTotal
columns.description: The total amount of workers.

name: LAJobLogs
description: Provides information about jobs executions (e.g. Export Job) within Log Analytics workspace. Including job status, duration, and errors.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time (UTC) of each status change in the job.

columns.name: JobType
columns.description: The type of the job. e.g. 'Export'.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The correlation ID of the Job action. Can be used to reference with logs from other tables, like AzureActivity, or included in support cases.

columns.name: JobId
columns.description: The ID of the job. Can be used as operationId in the operation resource URI (in request response) to get the job’s status or cancel a job.

columns.name: SourceTable
columns.description: The table(s) used in the job's query.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The job's status, including 'Started', 'Succeeded', 'Canceled, 'Failed'.

columns.name: ResultsRecordCount
columns.description: The number of records in the job's result.

columns.name: ResultsGB
columns.description: The results volume in Gigabytes. e.g. the number of Gigabytes exported to Storage Account.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: A message describing the job's operation or error.

columns.name: Destination
columns.description: The destination information based on the JobType. e.g. 'Export' includes the export target container name and storage Accounts.

name: LAQueryLogs
description: Audit logs for queries executed in Log Analytics Workspaces.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) at which the query was submitted.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID for correlated events.

columns.name: AADObjectId
columns.description: AAD ObjectId of the caller, or the 'aai' claim for GDAP scenarios.

columns.name: AADTenantId
columns.description: AAD TenantId of the caller.

columns.name: AADEmail
columns.description: AAD Email of the caller.

columns.name: AADClientId
columns.description: AAD ClientId used by the caller.

columns.name: ConditionalDataAccess
columns.description: Indicates whether the query was executed with an applicable attribute-based access control data access condition (ABAC), and if the condition had an error.

columns.name: QueryTimeRangeStart
columns.description: The starting time (UTC) of the time range across which the query was was requested by the caller to be executed.

columns.name: QueryTimeRangeEnd
columns.description: The end time (UTC) of the time range across which the query was was requested by the caller to be executed.

columns.name: QueryText
columns.description: The full body of the query as submitted by the user.

columns.name: QueryThumbprint
columns.description: A hash representing the query structure, useful for identifying similar queries.

columns.name: RequestClientApp
columns.description: ClientApp string in the request header (x-ms-app).

columns.name: RequestTarget
columns.description: ResourceId of the request URL.

columns.name: RequestContext
columns.description: ResourceId of all referenced workspaces, applications, and resources across which the query was requested by the caller to be executed.

columns.name: RequestContextFilters
columns.description: Filters applied to the request context.

columns.name: ResponseCode
columns.description: The HTTP response code for the request.

columns.name: ResponseRowCount
columns.description: The number of rows that were returned.

columns.name: ResponseDurationMs
columns.description: The duration (in ms) that the query took to execute.

columns.name: StatsCPUTimeMs
columns.description: The CPU (in ms) used in the execution of this query.

columns.name: StatsDataProcessedStart
columns.description: The starting time (UTC) of the time range across which the data processed.

columns.name: StatsDataProcessedEnd
columns.description: The end time (UTC) of the time range across which the data processed.

columns.name: StatsWorkspaceCount
columns.description: The number of workspaces that the query accessed, either explicitly or otherwise.

columns.name: StatsRegionCount
columns.description: The number of regions that the workspaces accessed are spread across.

columns.name: IsBillableQuery
columns.description: Indicates whether query execution is billed.

columns.name: ScannedGB
columns.description: For billable queries, like Basic logs queries, indicates the total GB of data scanned in the query.

columns.name: WorkspaceRegion
columns.description: The region of the queried workspace.

columns.name: IsWorkspaceInFailover
columns.description: Indicates whether the queried workspace was in failover mode.

name: LASummaryLogs
description: Provides Summary logs rules execution details, including run status, duration and errors. Can be used to view bins executions statuses, identify rules that take a long time to complete, and failures that could be optimized in query, or shorted bin time.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The bin execution start time (UTC). It represents the time bin processing started, and decoupled from bin time range. For example, TimeGenerated can be 2023-01-01T3:05:10.123Z when processing hourly bin between 2:00 - 3:00.

columns.name: BinStartTime
columns.description: The bin start time (UTC). For example, value can be 2023-01-01T2:00:00.000Z for bin processed between 2:00 - 3:00.

columns.name: BinSize
columns.description: The time ranges summarization is performed in minutes. For example, when bin is 60, summarization is performed every 60 minutes.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The bin execution status. Can be Started, Succeeded or Failed.

columns.name: QueryDurationMs
columns.description: The execution duration in milliseconds.

columns.name: ResultsRecordCount
columns.description: The number of records returned in aggregation.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: An error message when applicable.

columns.name: RuleName
columns.description: The rule name.

columns.name: RuleLastModifiedTime
columns.description: The time the rule last modified. Can be used to reason changes in results, duration, etc.

description: This table includes logs for cache events. Can be used for performance metrics.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the log was created.

columns.name: HitRatioMbps
columns.description: Ratio of Data volume (MB) per second that came directly from Microsoft Connected Cache(hitMbps) to The total data volume (MB) per second delivered(egressMbps).

columns.name: CacheNodeId
columns.description: Unique CacheNode identifier.

columns.name: MissMbps
columns.description: Data volume (MB) per second that Microsoft Connected Cache had to download from CDN to see the cache.

columns.description: Data volume (MB) per second that came directly from Microsoft Connected Cache.

columns.name: Hits
columns.description: The number of times data is found in the cache.

columns.name: Misses
columns.description: The number of times data is not found in the cache and had to download from CDN.

columns.name: EgressMbps
columns.description: The total data volume (MB) per second delivered including: data volume (MB) that came directly from cache (hitMbps) and data volume (MB) that Microsoft Connected Cache had to download from CDN to see the cache (missMbps).

columns.name: RequestId
columns.description: A unique identifier that identifies the request and response events for one specific request-response pair.

columns.name: IsRequest
columns.description: Whether this event is for the request or the response. True if this event is for the request. False if this event is for the response.

columns.name: Method
columns.description: The HTTP verb for this request.

columns.name: Url
columns.description: The full url for this request. This will contain the scheme, host, port (if applicable), path, and query string.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP response code of the response for this request.

columns.name: Payload
columns.description: A chunk of payload for the request or the response in this event. The full payload for the request and/or the response may be split into multiple chunks.

columns.name: PayloadLength
columns.description: The length of the payload chunk in this event. If there is no payload, this value will be zero.

columns.name: ChunkNum
columns.description: The index of the payload chunk. Starts from zero. If the full payload is broken into chunks, this value represents which chunk is in this event.

columns.name: IsLastChunk
columns.description: Whether the payload chunk in this event is the last one or not. True if this is the last chunk; false otherwise.

columns.name: PayloadType
columns.description: The value of the Content-Type header, if present.

columns.name: PayloadEncoding
columns.description: The value of the Content-Encoding header, if present.

columns.name: AuthenticationTypes
columns.description: A list of well-known authentication mechanisms that were detected, e.g., Authorization header or API keys.

columns.name: ContainsAuthentication
columns.description: Whether a well-known authentication mechanism was detected. True if any authentication mechanism was detected, false otherwise.

columns.name: ClientIPs
columns.description: A list of client IPs extracted from the X-Forwarded-For header in the request.

columns.name: ContainsPublicIp
columns.description: Whether one of the IPs extracted from the X-Forwarded-For header in the request is a public IP.

columns.name: Elapsed
columns.description: The time in milliseconds that have elapsed between the request and the response events.

columns.name: AdditionalMetadata
columns.description: Metadata related to the event.

columns.name: DataPipelineMetadata
columns.description: Metadata related to the data processing pipeline.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time (UTC) when the monitored entity was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: AzureResourceId
columns.description: The Azure resource ID of the resource whose monitored entity was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: AADTenantID
columns.description: The AAD tenant ID of the subscription in which the monitored entity was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: The name of the machine on which the monitored entity was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: CloudProvider
columns.description: The cloud provider of the resource.

columns.name: CloudIdentifier
columns.description: The cloud identifier of the resource.

columns.name: CloudResourceType
columns.description: The type of the cloud resource.

columns.name: MonitoredEntityType
columns.description: The type of the monitored entity that was created, renamed, modified or deleted. Can be either 'File' or 'Registry'.

columns.name: ChangeType
columns.description: The type of change that occurred on the entity. For 'File' entity must be either 'Created', 'Modified', 'Renamed' or 'Deleted'. For 'Registry' entity must be either 'RegistryKeyCreated', 'RegistryKeyDeleted', 'RegistryValueSet',  'RegistryValueDeleted', 'RegistryKeyRenamed'.

columns.name: FileName
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the name of the file that was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: FileType
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the type of the file that was created, renamed, modified or deleted. Example of possible values: Zip, PDF, Xar etc.

columns.name: FilePath
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the path of the file that was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: FileSize
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the current size (in bytes) of the file that was created, renamed, modified or deleted.

columns.name: OriginalFileName
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type and for a 'Rename' change type. Holds the original name the file that was renamed, before the  rename occured.

columns.name: OriginalFilePath
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type and for a 'Rename' change type. Holds the original path of the file that was renamed, before the  rename occured.

columns.name: FileMd5
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the MD5 of the file that was modified, created or deleted.

columns.name: FileSha256
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the SHA256 of the file that was modified, created or deleted.

columns.name: FileSha1
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the SHA1 of the file that was modified, created or deleted.

columns.name: RequestAccountName

columns.name: RequestAccountDomain

columns.name: RequestAccountSid
columns.description: Relevant for 'File' monitored entity type. Holds the SID of the account of the user that caused the file event.

columns.name: RequestSourceIP

columns.name: RequestSourcePort

columns.name: RequestSource

columns.description: Folder containing the process (image file) that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitProcessId
columns.description: Process ID (PID) of the process that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitProcessIntegrityLevel
columns.description: Integrity level of the process that initiated the event. Windows assigns integrity levels to processes based on certain characteristics, such as if they were launched from an internet download. These integrity levels influence permissions to resources.

columns.name: InitProcessMD5
columns.description: MD5 hash of the process (image file) that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitProcessParentFileName
columns.description: Name of the parent process that spawned the process responsible for the event.

columns.name: InitProcessParentId
columns.description: Process ID (PID) of the parent process that spawned the process responsible for the event.

columns.name: InitProcessSHA1
columns.description: SHA-1 hash of the process (image file) that initiated the event.

columns.name: InitProcessSHA256
columns.description: SHA-256 hash of the process (image file) that initiated the event. This field is usually not populated - use the SHA1 column when available.

columns.name: InitProcessTokenElevation
columns.description: Token type indicating the presence or absence of User Access Control (UAC) privilege elevation applied to the process that initiated the event.

columns.name: IsAzureInfoProtectionApplied
columns.description: Indicates whether the file is encrypted by Azure Information Protection.

columns.name: MD5
columns.description: MD5 hash of the file that the recorded action was applied to.

columns.name: MachineGroup
columns.description: Machine group of the machine. This group is used by role-based access control to determine access to the machine.

columns.name: PreviousFileName

columns.name: PreviousFolderPath
columns.description: Original folder containing the file before the recorded action was applied.

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: Event identifier based on a repeating counter. To identify unique events, this column must be used in conjunction with the ComputerName and EventTime columns.

columns.name: RequestAccountDomain

columns.name: RequestAccountName
columns.description: User name of account used to remotely initiate the activity.

columns.name: RequestAccountSid

columns.name: SHA256

columns.description: Sublabel applied to an email, file, or other content to classify it for information protection; sensitivity sublabels are grouped under sensitivity labels but are treated independently.

columns.name: InitProcessCreationTime

columns.name: InitProcessFileSize
columns.description: Size in bytes of the process (image file) that initiated the event.

name: NSPAccessLogs
description: Logs of Network Security Perimeter (NSP) inbound access allowed based on NSP access rules.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp marking the start of event aggregation.

columns.name: TimeGeneratedEndTime
columns.description: Timestamp indicating the end of event aggregation.

columns.name: Count
columns.description: Total number of aggregated events.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Indicates top-level PaaS operation name.

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: The API version associated with the operation.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: NSP access log categories.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Indicates the region of NSP.

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: Additional description about the operation result, when available.

columns.name: Profile
columns.description: Name of the NSP profile associated to the resource.

columns.name: Parameters
columns.description: List of optional PaaS resource properties in key-value pair format. For example: [ {Param1}: {value1}, {Param2}: {value2}, ...].

columns.name: ServiceResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of PaaS resource emitting NSP access logs.

columns.name: ServiceFqdn
columns.description: Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) of PaaS resource emitting NSP access logs.

columns.name: AppId
columns.description: Unique GUID representing the app ID of resource in the Azure Active Directory.

columns.name: MatchedRule
columns.description: JSON property bag containing matched access rule name. It can be either NSP access rule name or resource rule name (not it's resource ID).

columns.name: SourceResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of source PaaS resource for an inbound connection, when available.

columns.name: SourceIpAddress
columns.description: IP address of source making inbound connection, when available.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: Port number of inbound connection, when available.

columns.name: SourceProtocol
columns.description: Application layer protocol and transport layer protocol used for inbound connection in the format {AppProtocol}:{TransportProtocol}. For example: 'HTTPS:TCP'. It be must specified if available.

columns.name: SourcePerimeterGuids
columns.description: List of perimeter GUIDs of source resource. It should be specified only if allowed based on perimeter GUID.

columns.name: SourceAppId
columns.description: Unique GUID representing the app ID of source in the Azure Active Directory.

columns.name: SourceParameters
columns.description: List of optional source properties in key-value pair format. For example: [ {Param1}: {value1}, {Param2}: {value2}, ...].

columns.name: DestinationResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of destination PaaS resource for an outbound connection, when available.

columns.name: DestinationFqdn
columns.description: Fully Qualified Domain(FQDN) name of the destination.

columns.name: DestinationParameters
columns.description: List of optional destination properties in key-value pair format. For example: [ {Param1}: {value1}, {Param2}: {value2}, ...].

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: Port number of outbound connection, when available.

columns.name: DestinationProtocol
columns.description: Application layer protocol and transport layer protocol used for outbound connection in the format {AppProtocol}:{TransportProtocol}. For example: 'HTTPS:TCP'. It be must specified if available.

columns.name: DestinationEmailAddress
columns.description: Email address of destination receiver. It be must specified if available.

columns.name: DestinationPhoneNumber
columns.description: Phone number of destination receiver. It be must specified if available.

columns.name: AccessRuleVersion
columns.description: Access rule version of the NSP profile to which PaaS resource is associated.

columns.name: ResultDirection
columns.description: Direction of evaluation result whether 'Inbound' or 'Outbound'.

columns.name: ResultAction
columns.description: Indicates whether the result of evaluation is 'Approved' or 'Denied'.

columns.name: RuleType
columns.description: Indicates where the rule is defined: NSP or PaaS resource.

columns.name: TrafficType
columns.description: Indicates whether traffic is 'Private', 'Public', 'Intra' or 'Cross' perimeter.
