name: NTAInsights
description: Traffic Analytics insights are provided for flow data which shows anomalies in data pattern.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time when the data gets ingested into the Log Analytics Workspace.

columns.name: SubType
columns.description: Subtype for the insights logs.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: Schema version.

columns.name: AggregationType
columns.description: Type of data aggregation - 1 for short aggregation and 2 for long aggregation.

columns.name: IntervalStart
columns.description: Start time of the data insights processing interval.

columns.name: IntervalEnd
columns.description: End time of the data insights processing interval.

columns.name: PivotType
columns.description: Pivot type for insights aggregation.

columns.name: InsightsResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID for the resource for which data is aggregated

columns.name: Region
columns.description: Region of Vnet flow logs.

columns.name: DataPoints
columns.description: Data points for aggregated data.

columns.name: TrafficVolumeUnit
columns.description: The aggregated values represent Flows/Bytes/Packets.

columns.name: FlowStatus
columns.description: The considered traffic is Allowed/Denied/All.

columns.name: SeriesNumber
columns.description: An incremental value to represent the last aggregated series.

columns.name: ProcessingTime
columns.description: The time when periodicty is calculated.

columns.name: Periodicity
columns.description: The number of hours after whichthe data repeats itself.

columns.name: TrafficVolumeActual
columns.description: The actual traffic volume in the time period.

columns.name: TrafficVolumeBaseline
columns.description: The predicted value of the series, according to the decomposition per the anomaly calculation algorithm.

columns.name: TrafficTime
columns.description: Time when the anomaly has occured in data pattern.

columns.name: AdFlag
columns.description: A ternary series containing (+1, -1, 0) marking up/down/no anomaly respectively.

columns.name: AdScore
columns.description: Anomaly score.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: NTAIpDetails
description: Traffic Analytics provides WHOIS data and geographic location for all public IPs in the customer's environment. For Malicious IP, it provides DNS domain, threat type and thread descriptions as identified by Microsoft security intelligence solutions. IP Details are published to your Log Analytics Workspace so you can create custom queries and put alerts on them. You can also access pre-populated queries from the traffic analytics dashboard.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time when the data gets ingested into the Log Analytics Workspace.

columns.name: SubType
columns.description: Subtype for the flow logs. Use only FlowLog, other values of SubType_s are for internal workings of the product.

columns.name: FaSchemaVersion
columns.description: Schema version.

columns.name: FlowIntervalStartTime
columns.description: Start time of the flow log processing interval. This is time from which flow interval is measured.

columns.name: FlowIntervalEndTime
columns.description: End time of the flow log processing interval.

columns.name: FlowType
columns.description: Can be AzurePublic/ExternalPublic/MaliciousFlow.

columns.name: Ip
columns.description: Public IP whose information is provided in the record.

columns.name: PublicIpDetails
columns.description: For AzurePublic IP: Azure Service owning the IP OR "Microsoft Virtual Public IP" for IP 168.63.129.16 . ExternalPublic/Malicious IP: WhoIS information of the IP.

columns.name: ThreatType
columns.description: For Malicious IPs only: One of the threats from the list of currently allowed values.

columns.name: DnsDomain
columns.description: For Malicious IPs only: Domain name associated with this IP.

columns.name: ThreatDescription
columns.description: For Malicious IPs only: Description of the threat posed by the malicious IP.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: For Azure Public IP: Azure region of virtual network/network interface/virtual machine to which the IP belongs OR Global for IP 168.63.129.16. For External Public IP and Malicious IP: 2-letter country code where IP is located (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).

columns.name: Url
columns.description: For Malicious IPs only: Url associated with this IP.

columns.name: Port
columns.description: For Malicious IPs only: Port associated with this IP.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: NTANetAnalytics
description: Traffic Analytics records for Flowlog enriched data.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time when the data gets ingested into the log analytics workspace.

columns.name: SubType
columns.description: Subtype of the ingestion. Values can be Flowlog and StatusMessage.

columns.name: FaSchemaVersion
columns.description: Schema version for ingestion.

columns.name: TimeProcessed
columns.description: Time(in UTC) at which the traffic analytics processed the raw flow logs from the storage account.

columns.name: FlowIntervalStartTime
columns.description: Starting time(in UTC) of the flow log processing interval. This is time from which flow interval is measured.

columns.name: FlowIntervalEndTime
columns.description: Ending time(in UTC) of the flow log processing interval.

columns.name: FlowType
columns.description: Category of the flows(allowed values are IntraVNet, InterVNet, S2S, P2S, AzurePublic, ExternalPublic, MaliciousFlow, Unknown Private, Unknown) based on IP addresses involved in flow.

columns.name: FlowStartTime
columns.description: First occurrence of the flow (which will get aggregated) in the flow log processing interval between "FlowIntervalStartTime_t" and "FlowIntervalEndTime_t".

columns.name: FlowEndTime
columns.description: Last occurrence of the flow (which will get aggregated) in the flow log processing interval between "FlowIntervalStartTime_t" and "FlowIntervalEndTime_t". In terms of flow log v2, this field contains the time when the last flow with the same four-tuple started (marked as "B" in the raw flow record).

columns.name: SrcIp
columns.description: Source IP address.

columns.name: DestIp
columns.description: Destination IP address.

columns.name: SrcPorts
columns.description: Pipe Separated Source Ports.

columns.name: DestPort
columns.description: Destination port.

columns.name: L4Protocol
columns.description: Transport Protocol,T = TCP, U = UDP.

columns.name: L7Protocol
columns.description: Application Layer protocol name.

columns.name: IsFlowCapturedAtUdrHop
columns.description: True if flow gets captured at a UDR hop.

columns.name: FlowDirection
columns.description: Direction of the flow which can be inbound or outbound.

columns.name: FlowStatus
columns.description: Status of flow which can be allowed or denied.

columns.name: NsgList
columns.description: Network Security Group(NSG) associated with the flow. This is a placeholder for NSG flow logging.

columns.name: NsgRule
columns.description: NSG rule that allowed or denied this flow. This is a placeholder for NSG flow logging.

columns.name: NsgRuleType
columns.description: The type of Network Security Group(NSG) rule used by the flow. This is a placeholder for NSG flow logging.

columns.name: MacAddress
columns.description: MAC address of the network interface at which the flow was captured.

columns.name: TargetResourceId
columns.description: Target Resource Id for the resource where flow logging has been enabled.

columns.name: TargetResourceType
columns.description: Target Resource Type where flow logging is enabled. It can be virtual network(VNET)/subnet(SUBNET)/network interface(NIC).

columns.name: FlowLogResourceId
columns.description: The resource Id for the flow log

columns.name: SrcSubscription
columns.description: Subscription of the Azure virtual network/ network interface/ virtual machine to which the source IP in the flow belongs to.

columns.name: DestSubscription
columns.description: Subscription Id of virtual network/ network interface/ virtual machine to which the destination IP in the flow belongs to.

columns.name: SrcRegion
columns.description: Azure region of virtual network/ network interface/ virtual machine to which the source IP in the flow belongs to.

columns.name: DestRegion
columns.description: Azure region of virtual network/ network interface/ virtual machine to which the destination IP in the flow belongs to.

columns.name: SrcNic
columns.description: NIC associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestNic
columns.description: NIC associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcVm
columns.description: Virtual machine associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestVm
columns.description: Virtual machine associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcSubnet
columns.description: Subnet associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestSubnet
columns.description: Subnet associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcApplicationGateway
columns.description: Application gateway associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestApplicationGateway
columns.description: Application gateway associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcLoadBalancer
columns.description: Load balancer associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestLoadBalancer
columns.description: Load balancer associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcLocalNetworkGateway
columns.description: Local network gateway associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestLocalNetworkGateway
columns.description: Local network gateway associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcExpressRouteCircuit
columns.description: Express route circuit associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestExpressRouteCircuit
columns.description: Express route circuit associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: ExpressRouteCircuitPeeringType
columns.description: The peering type of the associated express route circuit for the flow.

columns.name: ConnectionType
columns.description: Type of the connection. Possible values are VNetPeering, VpnGateway, and ExpressRoute.

columns.name: ConnectionName
columns.description: Connection Name.

columns.name: ConnectingVnets
columns.description: Space separated list of virtual network names.

columns.name: Country
columns.description: Two letter country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).

columns.name: AzureRegion
columns.description: Azure region locations.

columns.name: FlowEncryption
columns.description: The type of flow encryption.

columns.name: AllowedInFlows
columns.description: Count of inbound flows that were allowed. This represents the number of flows that shared the same four-tuple inbound to the network interface at which the flow was captured.

columns.name: DeniedInFlows
columns.description: Count of inbound flows that were denied(Inbound to the network interface at which the flow was captured).

columns.name: AllowedOutFlows
columns.description: Count of outbound flows that were allowed(Outbound to the network interface at which the flow was captured).

columns.name: DeniedOutFlows
columns.description: Count of outbound flows that were denied(Outbound to the network interface at which the flow was captured).

columns.name: PacketsDestToSrc
columns.description: Represents packets sent from the destination to the source of the flow.

columns.name: PacketsSrcToDest
columns.description: Represents packets sent from the source to the destination of the flow.

columns.name: BytesDestToSrc
columns.description: Represents bytes sent from the destination to the source of the flow.

columns.name: BytesSrcToDest
columns.description: Represents bytes sent from the source to the destination of the flow.

columns.name: CompletedFlows
columns.description: This is populated with non-zero value when a flow gets a Completed event.

columns.name: SrcPublicIps
columns.description: Source public IP addresses flow information.

columns.name: DestPublicIps
columns.description: Destination public IP addresses flow information.

columns.name: AclGroup
columns.description: Access control list group refers to the network security group associated with the network security group rule name (or) the network group associated with the security admin configuration which allowed or denied the connection.

columns.name: AclRule
columns.description: Access control list rule refers to the network security group rule name or the security admin rule name which allowed or denied the connection.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Status of the ingestion. Possible values can be Completed, Partial or Failed.

columns.name: PrivateEndpointResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of the private endpoint resource.

columns.name: PrivateLinkResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of the private link service.

columns.name: PrivateLinkResourceName
columns.description: Resource name of the private link service.

columns.name: SrcServiceId
columns.description: Service ID associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestServiceId
columns.description: Service ID associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcServiceName
columns.description: Service Name associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestServiceName
columns.description: Service Name associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcTenantId
columns.description: Tenant ID associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestTenantId
columns.description: Tenant ID associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcTenantName
columns.description: Tenant Name associated with the source IP in the flow.

columns.name: DestTenantName
columns.description: Tenant Name associated with the destination IP in the flow.

columns.name: SrcEnvironment
columns.description: Environment associated with the source IP in the flow. Possible values can be Prod and NonProd.

columns.name: DestEnvironment
columns.description: Environment associated with the destination IP in the flow. Possible values can be Prod and NonProd.

columns.name: SrcCloud
columns.description: Cloud associated with the source IP in the flow. Possible values can be Public, Mooncake and Fairfax.

columns.name: DestCloud
columns.description: Cloud associated with the source IP in the flow. Possible values can be Public, Mooncake and Fairfax.

columns.name: SrcNetworkGroups
columns.description: In case source is tagged with a vnet, this is networkgroups that vnet belongs to.

columns.name: DestNetworkGroups
columns.description: In case destination is tagged with a vnet, this is networkgroups that vnet belongs to.

columns.name: SrcServiceTags
columns.description: Service tag of SrcPublicIps in format <Space separated list of source public ips grouped by service tag>|<ServiceTag>

columns.name: DestServiceTags
columns.description: Service tag of DestPublicIps in format <Space separated list of destination public ips grouped by service tag>|<ServiceTag>

columns.name: LogResourceId
columns.description: The resource ID of the resource responsible for the log generation. Eg. flow log ID for VNet flow logs, firewall diagnostic log ID for firewall logs, etc.

columns.name: Action
columns.description: Action taken for the record. For network flow records this would indicate whether the flow was allowed or denied. For example: Allow/Deny for Firewall logs.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Additional information about the record, if required. For Firewall logs this would contain the ActionReason from the firewall.

columns.name: TrafficAnalyticsResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of the Traffic Analytics resource that is responsible for Traffic Analytics processing for this log source.

columns.name: RecordCount
columns.description: Count of individual records that were aggregated into this record.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: NTARuleRecommendation
description: Traffic Analytics rules are recommended based on flow data for various pre-defined rules.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time when the data gets ingested into the Log Analytics Workspace.

columns.name: RecommendedRuleName
columns.description: The name of the rule being recommended.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: Version of the schema used for this record.

columns.name: SummarizationType
columns.description: Indicates how the flows were summarized by time. Possible values are Hourly, Daily.

columns.name: StartTime
columns.description: Start time of the flow observation window.

columns.name: EndTime
columns.description: End time of the flow observation window.

columns.name: RuleScope
columns.description: Scope within which the rule applies. Possible values are SubscriptionId, VirtualNetwork.

columns.name: TargetResourceId
columns.description: The resource targeted by the rule.

columns.name: TargetResourceIdsList
columns.description: List of all targeted resource IDs covered by the rule.

columns.name: SrcSubscriptionId
columns.description: Subscription ID of the target resource id sending the traffic.

columns.name: DestSubscriptionId
columns.description: Subscription ID of the target resource id receiving the traffic.

columns.name: L4Protocol
columns.description: Layer 4 protocol used in the traffic. Possible values are TCP, UDP.

columns.name: PortCategory
columns.description: Indicates the classification of the destination port based on well-known or commonly used port ranges.

columns.name: SrcPublicIpCidrs
columns.description: Public source IPs in CIDR format for inbound flows.

columns.name: DestPublicIpCidrs
columns.description: Public destination IPs in CIDR format for outbound flows.

columns.name: SrcServiceTagsList
columns.description: Service tags associated with source traffic for inbound flows.

columns.name: DestServiceTagsList
columns.description: Service tags associated with destination traffic for outbound flows.

columns.name: TotalFlowCount
columns.description: Total number of flows observed for this rule.

columns.name: DestPortsRanges
columns.description: Comma-separated list of destination port ranges on target resource id.

columns.name: IpRegionDetails
columns.description: Region information for the involved IP addresses.

columns.name: IpUrls
columns.description: List of Urls for the malicious Ips.

columns.name: UnecryptedFlowDetails
columns.description: For unencrypted flow, it specifies the encryption level. Possible values are Unencrypted, Unsupported hardware, Software not ready, Drop due to no encryption, Discovery not supported, Destination on same host, Fall back to no encryption.

columns.name: RecommendedAction
columns.description: Recommended action on the recommended rule. Possible values are Allow, Block, Advisory.

columns.name: VirtualNetworkResourceId
columns.description: Virtual network name targeted by rule.

columns.name: AdditionalProperties
columns.description: Placeholder for additional properties related to the recommended rule.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: NTATopologyDetails
description: Traffic Analytics records for Topology data.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The time when the data gets ingested into the log analytics workspace.

columns.name: SubType
columns.description: Subtype of the ingestion. Values can be Topology and StatusMessage.

columns.name: TimeProcessed
columns.description: Time(in UTC) at which the traffic analytics discovered the topology resource.

columns.name: TopologyVersion
columns.description: Topology version.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: This is topology schema version and not related to flow log schema version.

columns.name: MacAddress
columns.description: MAC address of the discovered NIC.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: Name of the discovered resource.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: Region of the discovered resource.

columns.name: AzureResourceType
columns.description: Resource type of the discovered resource.

columns.name: Subscription
columns.description: Subscription guid of the discovered resource.

columns.name: SubscriptionName
columns.description: Subscription name of the discovered resource.

columns.name: DiscoveryRegion
columns.description: The region where resource is discovered.

columns.name: Tags
columns.description: Tags associated with the discovered resource.

columns.name: AddressPrefixes
columns.description: The address prefixes associated with the discovered resource.

columns.name: Nsg
columns.description: The reference to the network security group resource.

columns.name: RouteTable
columns.description: The reference to the route table resource.

columns.name: GatewayType
columns.description: Gateway Type assocaited with virtual network gateway, VPN or express route.

columns.name: VirtualSubnetwork
columns.description: Virtual subnetwork associated with virtual network gateway.

columns.name: VipAddress
columns.description: Space separated list of IP addresses associated with virtual network gateway.

columns.name: Sku
columns.description: SKU or pricing associated with the discovered resource.

columns.name: BgpEnabled
columns.description: Whether BGP is enabled for this resource or not.

columns.name: VpnClientAddressPrefixes
columns.description: The reference to the address prefix resource which represents address prefix for P2S VpnClient. Will be empty when no point to site is configured.

columns.name: IpAddress
columns.description: Gateway IP address of the discovered resource.

columns.name: SubnetPrefixes
columns.description: Space separated string of address prefixes in local network address space.

columns.name: IsVirtualAppliance
columns.description: Boolean to specify if the discovered resource is a virtual appliance.

columns.name: VmssName
columns.description: The virtual machine scale set name.

columns.name: Zones
columns.description: The virtual machine zones information.

columns.name: Priority
columns.description: Specifies the priority for the virtual machine. Minimum api-version: 2019-03-01.

columns.name: VirtualMachine
columns.description: The reference to a virtual machine.

columns.name: PrivateIpAddresses
columns.description: Private IP address of the IP configuration.

If the flag is set to true, and allowGatewayTransit on remote peering is also true, virtual network will use gateways of remote virtual network for transit. Only one peering can have this flag set to true. This flag cannot be set if virtual network already has a gateway.

description: Table of logs related to traffic through NAT Gateway resources.

columns.description: Date and time the record was generated.

columns.name: SourceIP
columns.description: The source IP generating the traffic through the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: DestinationIP
columns.description: The destination IP the traffic is getting forwarded to, through the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: NatGatewayIP
columns.description: The NAT Gateway IP address.

columns.name: PacketsReceived
columns.description: Count of packets received from the destination IP and allowed by the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: PacketsSent
columns.description: Count of packets sent from the source IP and allowed by the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: BytesReceived
columns.description: Sum of the size (in bytes) of all the packets received from the destination IP and allowed by the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: BytesSent
columns.description: Sum of the size (in bytes) of all the packets sent from the source IP and allowed by the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: PacketsReceivedDropped
columns.description: Count of packets received from the destination IP and dropped at the NAT Gateway.

columns.name: PacketsSentDropped
columns.description: Count of packets sent from the source IP and dropped at the NAT Gateway.

name: NetworkAccessAlerts
description: This table is part of Identity and Network Access, which contains Network Access Alerts. These Alerts can be leveraged for knowing the state of your network access.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The date and time (UTC) that the event was generated.

columns.name: CreationDateTime
columns.description: The date and time (UTC) that the event was generated.

columns.name: FirstActivityDateTime
columns.description: The impact start time of the alert (the time of the first event or activity included in the alert). The field is serialized a string according to ISO8601, including UTC timezone information.

columns.name: LastActivityDateTime
columns.description: The impact end time of the alert (the time of the last event or activity included in the alert). The field is serialized a string according to ISO8601, including UTC timezone information.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: A unique identifier for each Network Access Alert.

columns.name: VendorName
columns.description: The name of the vendor that raised the alert, this value is displayed to users as is. For most internal security products alerts it should be set as ‘Microsoft’.

columns.name: ProductName
columns.description: The name of the product which published this alert, i.e. Azure Security Center, Azure ATP, Microsoft Defender ATP, O365 ATP, MCAS, etc.

columns.name: ComponentName
columns.description: The name of a component inside the product which generated the alert. This is an optional field, which might be populated only for product in which external end user are aware of specific components within a product. For products that offer different types of SKU/Bundles, this field can hold the SKU or bundle name.

columns.name: AlertType
columns.description: The type name of the alert. Alerts of the same type should have the same name. This field is a keyed string representing the type of alert and not of an alert instance. All alert instances from the same detection logic/analytic should have the same value for alert type.

columns.name: DisplayName
columns.description: The display name of the alert, this value is displayed to users either as-is or with additional parameters.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: The number of bytes sent from the source to the destination for the connection or session.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: The severity of the alert as it is reported by the provider. Possible Values: Informational, Low, Medium, High.

columns.name: Techniques
columns.description: Optional field that specify the kill chain related techniques behind the alert. Each technique should be added in this list using its ID and it should have at least one matching intent in the Intent field. The Production of this field (the expected format of the technique ID and the matching against the Intent values) follow MITRE att@ck enterprise matrix model (Opens in new window or tab), and further guidance on the different techniques that make up each intent can be found in MITRE's documentation.

columns.name: SubTechniques
columns.description: Optional field that specify the kill chain related sub-techniques behind the alert. Each sub-technique should be added in this list using its ID and it should have at least one matching intent in the Intent field.

columns.name: DetectionTechnology
columns.description: Optional field to hold the alert threat detection technology.

columns.name: PolicyId
columns.description: The Policy Id associated with the network access traffic that generated the Alert.

columns.name: IsPreview
columns.description: IsPreview will be defined as true where the alert is in public preview state and not eligible for GA yet. By default the value is false.

columns.name: ExtendedProperties
columns.description: A bag of fields which will be presented to the user. Providers can send here any custom fields that should be part of the alert.

columns.name: RelatedResources
columns.description: A list of entities related to the alert. This list can hold a mixture of entities of diverse types. The entities type can be any of the types defined in the Entities section. Entities which are not in the list below can also be sent, however it is not guaranteed that they will be processed (the alert will not fail Production with new types of entities).

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: NetworkAccessConnectionEvents

columns.name: DeviceOperatingSystemVersion

columns.name: AgentVersion

columns.name: ClientDeviceName

columns.name: NetworkProtocol

columns.name: RemoteNetworkId

columns.name: FlowType
columns.description: Type of the flow (e.g., L3_L4, L7 SOCK) (flow.Type).

columns.name: NodeName
columns.description: Name of the node where the flow was captured (flow.node_name).

columns.name: Layer7
columns.description: L7 flow type if Flow_Type is L7 (e.g., DNS, HTTP, Kafka) (flow.l7.type).

columns.name: Reply
columns.description: Indicates if the flow is a reply (flow.is_reply.value).

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Event type details (flow.event_type).

columns.name: Service
columns.description: Service details of the flow.

columns.name: TrafficDirection
columns.description: Direction of the traffic (e.g., INGRESS, EGRESS) (flow.traffic_direction).

columns.name: TraceObservationPoint
columns.description: Point of observation in the trace (e.g., TO_ENDPOINT) (flow.trace_observation_point).

columns.name: PacketsSent
columns.description: Number of packets sent from the source to the destination since the last update.

columns.name: PacketsReceived
columns.description: Number of packets sent from the destination to the source since the last update.

columns.name: Policies
columns.description: Combined entry for all policies that allowed or denied ingress/egress (flow.egress_allowed_by, flow.ingress_allowed_by, flow.egress_denied_by, flow.ingress_denied_by).

columns.name: AdditionalFlowData
columns.description: Additional flow data.

columns.name: LogicalServerName
columns.description: Logical server name.

columns.name: ResourceGroup
columns.description: Resource group of the SQL resoruce.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the log was generated.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: The category of the log.

columns.name: AuditSchemaVersion
columns.description: The audit logs schema version.

columns.name: EventTime
columns.description: The time (UTC) the event was fired at.

columns.name: SequenceNumber
columns.description: Tracks the sequence of records within a single audit record that was too large to fit in the write buffer for audits.

columns.name: ActionId
columns.description: ID of the audit action.

columns.name: ActionName
columns.description: The name of the audit action.

columns.name: Succeeded
columns.description: Indicates whether the action that triggered the event succeeded. Is not nullable. For all events other than login events, this only reports whether the permission check succeeded or failed, not the operation.

columns.name: IsColumnPermission
columns.description: Flag indicating if this is a column level permission.

columns.name: SessionId
columns.description: ID of the session on which the event occurred.

columns.name: ServerPrincipalId
columns.description: ID of the login context that the action is performed in.

columns.name: DatabasePrincipalId
columns.description: ID of the database user context that the action is performed in.

columns.name: TargetServerPrincipalId
columns.description: Server principal that the GRANT/DENY/REVOKE operation is performed on.

columns.name: TargetDatabasePrincipalId
columns.description: The database principal the GRANT/DENY/REVOKE operation is performed on.

columns.name: ObjectId
columns.description: The ID of the entity on which the audit occurred.

columns.name: UserDefinedEventId
columns.description: User defined event id passed as an argument to sp_audit_write.

columns.name: TransactionId
columns.description: Unique identifier to identify multiple audit events in one transaction.

columns.name: ClassType
columns.description: The type of auditable entity that the audit occurs on.

columns.name: ClassTypeDescription
columns.description: The description of the class type.

columns.name: SecurableClassType
columns.description: The type of auditable entity that the audit occurs on.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Query execution duration in milliseconds.

columns.name: ResponseRows
columns.description: Number of rows returned in the result set.

columns.name: AffectedRows
columns.description: Number of rows affected by the executed statement.

columns.name: ClientTlsVersion
columns.description: Client TLS version

columns.name: ClientIp
columns.description: Source IP of the client application

columns.name: PermissionBitmask
columns.description: In some actions, this is the permissions that were grant, denied, or revoked.

columns.name: SequenceGroupId
columns.description: Unique identifier.

columns.name: SessionServerPrincipalName
columns.description: Server principal for session. Is nullable. Returns the identity of the original login which was connected to the instance of SQL Server in case there were explicit or implicit context switches.

columns.name: ServerPrincipalName
columns.description: Current login. Is nullable.

columns.name: ServerPrincipalSid
columns.description: Current login SID.

columns.name: DatabasePrincipalName
columns.description: Current user.

columns.name: TargetServerPrincipalName
columns.description: Target login of action.

columns.name: TargetServerPrincipalSid
columns.description: SID of target login.

columns.name: TargetDatabasePrincipalName
columns.description: Target user of action.

columns.name: DatabaseName
columns.description: The database context in which the action occurred.

columns.name: SchemaName
columns.description: The schema context in which the action occurred.

columns.name: ObjectName
columns.description: The name of the entity on which the audit occurred.

columns.name: Statement
columns.description: TSQL statement if it exists.

columns.name: AdditionalInformation
columns.description: Unique information that only applies to a single event is returned as XML.

columns.name: UserDefinedInformation
columns.description: Used to record any extra information the user wants to record in audit log by using the sp_audit_write stored procedure.

columns.name: ApplicationName
columns.description: Name of client application which executed the statement that caused the audit event.

columns.name: ConnectionId
columns.description: ID of the connection in the server.

columns.name: DataSensitivityInformation
columns.description: Information types and sensitivity labels returned by the audited query, based on the classified columns in the database.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: The host name.

columns.name: SessionContext
columns.description: The Session context key value content. provided as an XML.

columns.name: IsServerLevelAudit
columns.description: Boolean indicating whether this was generated from a server level audit or database level audit.

columns.name: EventId
columns.description: unique Guid identifying each audit event.

name: SignalRServiceDiagnosticLogs
description: Azure SignalR service diagnostic logs.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of the log.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The operation name of the log event. it can be used to filter the log based on a specific operation name.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The location of Azure SignalR service.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: The level of the log. Can be 'Informational', 'Warning', 'Error' or 'Critical'.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: The IP of the client or server connects to SignalR service.

columns.name: Collection
columns.description: The collection of the log event. Can be 'Connection', 'Authorization', 'Throttling' or 'Message'. 'Connection' collection includes the logs about the lifetime of connections. 'Authorization' includes the logs about the authorization of connections. 'Throttling' includes the logs about the throttled connections. 'Message' includes the logs about the tracing messages.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: The message of the log event. It describes the log event in detail.

columns.name: HubName
columns.description: The SignalR Hubs API enables you to call methods on connected clients from the server.

columns.name: GroupName
columns.description: A group can have any number of clients, and a client can be a member of any number of groups.

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: The user ID of the connection. It is defined by the client or app server.

columns.name: ConnectionId
columns.description: The connection ID of the connection connected to SignalR service.

columns.name: ConnectionType
columns.description: The connection type. Can be 'Server' and 'Client'. 'Server' means the connection connects to an app server. 'Client' means the connection connects to a SignalR client.

columns.name: TransportType
columns.description: The transport type of the connection. Can be 'WebSockets', 'ServerSentEvents', or 'LongPolling'. For more details, see https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.aspnetcore.http.connections.httptransporttype.

columns.name: MessageType
columns.description: The type of the messsage. Can be 'BroadcastDataMessage', 'MultiConnectionDataMessage', 'GroupBroadcastDataMessage', 'MultiGroupBroadcastDataMessage', 'UserDataMessage', 'MultiUserDataMessage', 'JoinGroupWithAckMessage' and 'LeaveGroupWithAckMessage'. For more details, see https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.Protocols.

columns.name: MessageTracingId
columns.description: The tracing ID of the message. It's used for tracing messages.

columns.name: InvocationId
columns.description: The invocation ID of the message. It's only available in ASP.NET SignalR.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: ResourceId
columns.description: The identifier of the resource that the user signed in to.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Provides the 5-6 digit error code that's generated during a sign-in event. 0 indicates success; other values are failures. You can find more information using the Azure AD Error Codes documentation or https://login.microsoftonline.com/error.

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: Provides the error message or the reason for failure for the corresponding sign-in activity.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The identifier that's sent from the client when sign-in is initiated. This is used for troubleshooting the corresponding sign-in activity when calling for support.

columns.name: Identity
columns.description: The display name of the actor identified in the signin.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The 2 letter country code from where the sign-in occurred. Depending on IP address provided, this value may not always resolve to a city or region level of detail.

columns.name: AlternateSignInName
columns.description: The identification that the user provided to sign in. It may be the userPrincipalName but it's also populated when a user signs in using other identifiers.

columns.name: AppDisplayName
columns.description: The application name displayed in the Azure Portal.

columns.name: AppId
columns.description: The application identifier in Azure Active Directory.

columns.name: AuthenticationContextClassReferences
columns.description: Contains a collection of values that represent the conditional access authentication contexts applied to the sign-in.

columns.name: AuthenticationDetails
columns.description: The result of the authentication attempt and additional details on the authentication method.

columns.name: AppliedEventListeners
columns.description: Detailed information about the listeners, such as Azure Logic Apps and Azure Functions, that were triggered by the corresponding events in the sign-in event.

columns.name: AuthenticationMethodsUsed
columns.description: The authentication methods used. Possible values: SMS, Authenticator App, App Verification code, Password, FIDO, PTA, or PHS.

columns.name: AuthenticationProcessingDetails
columns.description: Additional authentication processing details, such as the agent name in case of PTA/PHS or Server/farm name in case of federated authentication.

columns.name: AuthenticationRequirement
columns.description: This holds the highest level of authentication needed through all the sign-in steps, for sign-in to succeed.

columns.name: AuthenticationRequirementPolicies
columns.description: Sources of authentication requirement, such as conditional access, per-user MFA, identity protection, and security defaults.

columns.name: ClientAppUsed
columns.description: The legacy client used for sign-in activity. For example: Browser, Exchange ActiveSync, Modern clients, IMAP, MAPI, SMTP, or POP.

columns.name: ConditionalAccessPolicies
columns.description: A list of conditional access policies that are triggered by the corresponding sign-in activity.

columns.name: ConditionalAccessStatus
columns.description: The status of the conditional access policy triggered. Possible values: success, failure, or notApplied.

columns.name: CreatedDateTime
columns.description: The date and time the sign-in was initiated. The Timestamp type is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

columns.name: DeviceDetail
columns.description: The device information from where the sign-in occurred. Includes information such as deviceId, OS, and browser.

columns.name: IsInteractive
columns.description: Indicates whether a user sign in is interactive. In interactive sign in, the user provides an authentication factor to Azure AD. These factors include passwords, responses to MFA challenges, biometric factors, or QR codes that a user provides to Azure AD or an associated app. In non-interactive sign in, the user doesn't provide an authentication factor. Instead, the client app uses a token or code to authenticate or access a resource on behalf of a user. Non-interactive sign ins are commonly used for a client to sign in on a user's behalf in a process transparent to the user.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: The identifier representing the sign-in activity.

columns.name: IPAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the client from where the sign-in occurred.

columns.name: LocationDetails
columns.description: Provides the city, state, country/region and latitude and longitude from where the sign-in happened.

columns.name: MfaDetail
columns.description: This property is deprecated.

columns.name: NetworkLocationDetails
columns.description: The network location details including the type of network used and its names.

columns.name: OriginalRequestId
columns.description: The request identifier of the first request in the authentication sequence.

columns.name: RiskDetail
columns.description: The reason behind a specific state of a risky user, sign-in, or a risk event. Possible values: none, adminGeneratedTemporaryPassword, userPerformedSecuredPasswordChange, userPerformedSecuredPasswordReset, adminConfirmedSigninSafe, aiConfirmedSigninSafe, userPassedMFADrivenByRiskBasedPolicy, adminDismissedAllRiskForUser, or adminConfirmedSigninCompromised. The value none means that no action has been performed on the user or sign-in so far. Note: Details for this property are only available for Azure AD Premium P2 customers. All other customers are returned hidden.

columns.name: RiskEventTypes
columns.description: This property is deprecated.

columns.name: RiskEventTypes_V2
columns.description: The list of risk event types associated with the sign-in. Possible values: unlikelyTravel, anonymizedIPAddress, maliciousIPAddress, unfamiliarFeatures, malwareInfectedIPAddress, suspiciousIPAddress, leakedCredentials, investigationsThreatIntelligence, or generic.

columns.name: RiskLevelAggregated
columns.description: The aggregated risk level. Possible values: none, low, medium, high, or hidden. The value hidden means the user or sign-in was not enabled for Azure AD Identity Protection. Note: Details for this property are only available for Azure AD Premium P2 customers. All other customers are returned hidden.

columns.name: RiskLevelDuringSignIn
columns.description: The risk level during sign-in. Possible values: none, low, medium, high, or hidden. The value hidden means the user or sign-in was not enabled for Azure AD Identity Protection. Note: Details for this property are only available for Azure AD Premium P2 customers. All other customers are returned hidden.

columns.name: RiskState
columns.description: The risk state of a risky user, sign-in, or a risk event. Possible values: none, confirmedSafe, remediated, dismissed, atRisk, or confirmedCompromised.

columns.name: ResourceDisplayName
columns.description: The name of the resource that the user signed in to.

columns.name: ResourceIdentity
columns.description: The resource that the user signed in to.

columns.name: ResourceServicePrincipalId
columns.description: The identifier of the service principal representing the target resource in the sign-in event.

columns.name: ServicePrincipalId
columns.description: The application identifier used for sign-in. This field is populated when you are signing in using an application.

columns.name: ServicePrincipalName
columns.description: The application name used for sign-in. This field is populated when you are signing in using an application.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The sign-in status. Includes the error code and description of the error (in case of a sign-in failure).

columns.name: TokenIssuerName
columns.description: The name of the identity provider. For example, sts.microsoft.com.

columns.name: TokenIssuerType
columns.description: The type of identity provider. The possible values are: AzureAD, or ADFederationServices, AzureADBackupAuth, ADFederationServicesMFAAdapter, NPSExtension.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The user agent information related to sign-in.

columns.name: UserDisplayName
columns.description: The display name of the user.

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: The identifier of the user.

columns.name: UserPrincipalName
columns.description: The UPN of the user.

columns.name: UserType
columns.description: Identifies whether the user is a member or guest in the tenant. Possible values are: member and guest.

columns.name: FlaggedForReview
columns.description: During a failed sign in, a user may click a button in the Azure portal to mark the failed event for tenant admins. If a user clicked the button to flag the failed sign in, this value is true.

columns.name: IPAddressFromResourceProvider
columns.description: The IP address a user used to reach a resource provider, used to determine Conditional Access compliance for some policies. For example, when a user interacts with Exchange Online, the IP address Exchange receives from the user may be recorded here. This value is often null.

columns.name: SignInIdentifier
columns.description: The identification that the user provided to sign in. It may be the userPrincipalName but it's also populated when a user signs in using other identifiers.

columns.name: SignInIdentifierType
columns.description: The type of sign in identifier. Possible values are: userPrincipalName, phoneNumber, proxyAddress, qrCode, onPremisesUserPrincipalName.

columns.name: ResourceTenantId
columns.description: The tenant identifier of the resource referenced in the sign in.

columns.name: HomeTenantId
columns.description: The tenant identifier of the user initiating the sign in. Not applicable in Managed Identity or service principal sign ins.

columns.name: UniqueTokenIdentifier
columns.description: A unique base64 encoded request identifier used to track tokens issued by Azure AD as they are redeemed at resource providers.

columns.name: SessionId
columns.description: Id of the session that was generated during the signIn.

columns.name: SessionLifetimePolicies
columns.description: Any conditional access session management policies that were applied during the sign-in event.

columns.name: AutonomousSystemNumber
columns.description: The Autonomous System Number (ASN) of the network used by the actor.

columns.name: AuthenticationProtocol
columns.description: Lists the protocol type or grant type used in the authentication. The possible values are: none, oAuth2, ropc, wsFederation, saml20, deviceCode. For authentications that use protocols other than the possible values listed, the protocol type is listed as none.

columns.name: CrossTenantAccessType
columns.description: Describes the type of cross-tenant access used by the actor to access the resource.

columns.name: AuthenticationAppDeviceDetails
columns.description: Details of the app and device state used during the most recent authentication step using an authentication app.

columns.name: AuthenticationAppPolicyEvaluationDetails
columns.description: The details of the policies applied and enforced related to the authentication app during the latest signIn step.

columns.name: ClientCredentialType
columns.description: The type of client credential used. Examples include client assertion, client secret, etc.

columns.name: FederatedCredentialId
columns.description: Federated Credential Id.

columns.name: GlobalSecureAccessIpAddress
columns.description: Global secure IP address that user signed in from.

columns.name: HomeTenantName
columns.description: The tenant name of the external tenant who homes the entitity taking action in the customer's tenant.

columns.name: IncomingTokenType
columns.description: The type of token utilized to signIn (examples: primary refresh token, saml assertion).

columns.name: IsTenantRestricted
columns.description: Indicates if a signIn is under a tenant restrictions policy or not.

columns.name: IsThroughGlobalSecureAccess
columns.description: Displays whether or not a user came through Global Secure Access service or not.

columns.name: OriginalTransferMethod
columns.description: Transfer method used to initiate a session throughout all subsequent requests.

columns.name: TokenProtectionStatusDetails
columns.description: Token protection creates a cryptographically secure tie between the token and the device it's issued to. This field indicates whether the signin token was bound to the device or not.

columns.name: AppOwnerTenantId
columns.description: The tenant identifier of the owenr of the application in Azure Active Directory.

columns.name: ResourceOwnerTenantId
columns.description: The tenant identifier of the owner of the resource referenced in the sign in.

columns.name: Agent
columns.description: The agentic property for sign in logs. Includes the agentType and the parentAppId when the type is AgenticInstance.

columns.name: SourceAppClientId
columns.description: The Source App's Client ID for Target Identities.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

name: StorageBlobLogs
description: Storage Blob Service Logs Schema

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The Universal Time Coordinated (UTC) time when the request was received by storage.

columns.name: AccountName
columns.description: The name of the storage account.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The location of storage account.

columns.name: Protocol
columns.description: The protocol that is used in the operation.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The type of REST operation that was performed.

columns.name: AuthenticationType
columns.description: The type of authentication that was used to make the request.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP status code for the request. If the request is interrupted, this value might be set to Unknown.

columns.name: StatusText
columns.description: The status of the requested operation.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: The total time, expressed in milliseconds, to perform the requested operation. This includes the time to read the incoming request, and to send the response to the requester.

columns.name: ServerLatencyMs
columns.description: The total time expressed in milliseconds to perform the requested operation. This value doesn't include network latency (the time to read the incoming request and send the response to the requester).

columns.name: Uri
columns.description: Uniform resource identifier that is requested.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address of the requester, including the port number.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The ID that is used to correlate logs across resources.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: The schema version of the log.

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: The storage service version that was specified when the request was made. This is equivalent to the value of the x-ms-version header.

columns.name: AuthenticationHash
columns.description: The hash of authentication token.

columns.name: RequesterObjectId
columns.description: The OAuth object ID of the requester.

columns.name: RequesterTenantId
columns.description: The OAuth tenant ID of identity.

columns.name: RequesterAppId
columns.description: The Open Authorization (OAuth) application ID that is used as the requester.

columns.name: RequesterAudience
columns.description: The OAuth audience of the request.

columns.name: RequesterTokenIssuer
columns.description: The OAuth token issuer.

columns.name: RequesterUpn
columns.description: The User Principal Names of requestor.

columns.name: AuthorizationDetails
columns.description: Detailed policy information used to authorize the request.

columns.name: UserAgentHeader
columns.description: The User-Agent header value, in quotes.

columns.name: ReferrerHeader
columns.description: The Referer header value.

name: SynapseDXFailedIngestion
description: Failed ingestion operations logs provide detailed information about failed ingest operations. Logs include data source details, as well as error code and failure status (transient or permanent), that can be used for tracking the process of data source ingestion. Users can identify usage errors (permanent bad requests) and handle retries of transient failures. Ingestion logs are supported for queued ingestion to the ingestion endpoint using SDKs, data connections, and connectors

columns.description: Free disk capacity of the primary disk in Megabytes.

columns.name: OSVersion
columns.description: The version of Windows 10 as is organized on aka.ms/win10releaseinfo.

columns.name: OSBuild
columns.description: The currently-installed Windows 10 Build in the format 'Major'.'Revision'. 'Major' corresponds to which Feature Update the device is on, whereas 'Revision' corresponds to which quality update the device is on. Mappings between Feature release and Major, as well as Revision and KBs, are available aka.ms/win10releaseinfo.

columns.name: OSBuildNumber
columns.description: An integer value for the revision number of the currently-installed Windows 10 OSBuild on the device.

columns.name: OSRevisionNumber
columns.description: An integer value for the revision number of the currently-installed Windows 10 OSBuild on the device.

columns.name: OSEdition
columns.description: The Windows 10 Edition or SKU.

columns.name: OSArchitecture
columns.description: The architecture of the Operating System e.g. x86.

columns.name: IsVirtual
columns.description: Whether device is a Virtual Device.

columns.name: OSServicingChannel
columns.description: The elected Windows 10 Servicing Channel of the device.

columns.name: OSFeatureUpdateStatus
columns.description: Whether or not the device is on the latest available Feature Update.

columns.name: OSQualityUpdateStatus
columns.description: Whether or not the device is on the latest available Quality Update, for its Feature Update.

columns.name: OSSecurityUpdateStatus
columns.description: Whether or not the device is on the latest available Security Update, for its Feature Update.

columns.name: OSFeatureUpdateComplianceStatus
columns.description: Whether or not the device is on the latest Feature Update being Offered by WUfB DS, else NotApplicable.

columns.name: OSSecurityUpdateComplianceStatus
columns.description: Whether or not the device is on the latest Security update (QU, Classification==Security) being offered by WUfB DS, else NotApplicable.

columns.name: OSQualityUpdateComplianceStatus
columns.description: Whether or not the device is on the latest Quality Update being Offered by WUfB DS, else NotApplicable.

columns.name: OSFeatureUpdateReleaseTime
columns.description: The release date of the Feature Update currently installed on the device.

columns.name: OSQualityUpdateReleaseTime
columns.description: The release date of the Quality Update currently installed on the device.

columns.name: OSFeatureUpdateEOSTime
columns.description: The end of service date of the Feature Update currently installed on the device.

columns.name: WUFeaturePauseState
columns.description: Indicates pause status of device for FU, possible values are Paused, NotPaused, NotConfigured.

columns.name: WUFeatureDeferralDays
columns.description: CSP: DeferFeatureUpdates. The WU Feature Update Deferral configuration in days. -1 indicates not configured, 0 indicates configured but set to 0. Values >0 indicate the policy setting.

columns.name: WUQualityDeferralDays
columns.description: CSP: DeferQualityUpdatesThe WU Quality Update Deferral configuration in days. -1 indicates not configured, 0 indicates configured but set to 0. Values >0 indicate the policy setting.

columns.name: WUQualityPauseState
columns.description: Indicates pause status of device for QU, possible values are Paused, NotPaused, NotConfigured.

columns.name: WUFeatureDeadlineDays
columns.description: CSP: ConfigureDeadlineForFeatureUpdatesThe WU Feature Update deadline configuration in days. -1 indicates not configured, 0 indicates configured but set to 0. Values >0 indicate the deadline in days.

columns.name: WUQualityDeadlineDays
columns.description: CSP: ConfigureDeadlineForQualityUpdates The WU Qualty Update deadline configuration in days. -1 indicates not configured, 0 indicates configured but set to 0. Values >0 indicate the deadline in days.

columns.name: WUFeatureGracePeriodDays
columns.description: The WU grace period for feature update in days. -1 indicates not configured, 0 indicates configured and set to 0. Values greater than 0 indicate the Grace Period in days.

columns.name: WUQualityGracePeriodDays
columns.description: The WU grace period for quality update in days. -1 indicates not configured, 0 indicates configured and set to 0. Values greater than 0 indicate the Grace Period in days.

columns.name: WUDODownloadMode
columns.description: The WU DO DownloadMode configuration, brought over from Update Compliance.

columns.name: UpdateConnectivityLevel
columns.description: Whether or not this device is maintaining a sufficiently cumulative and continuous connection to Windows Update so the update can progress optimally.

columns.name: WUFeaturePauseStartTime
columns.description: CSP: PauseFeatureUpdatesStartTime. The time WU Feature Update Pause was activated, if activated, else null.eature Updates will be paused for 35 days from the specified start date.

columns.name: WUFeaturePauseEndTime
columns.description: CSP:PauseFEatureUpdatesEndTime The time WU Feature Update Pause will end, if activated, else null.

columns.name: WUQualityPauseStartTime
columns.description: CSP:PauseQualityUpdatesStartTime The time WU Quality Update Pause was activated; if activated; else null.

columns.name: WUQualityPauseEndTime
columns.description: CSP:PauseQualityUpdatesEndTimeThe time WU Quality Update Pause will end, if activated, else null.

columns.name: WUAutomaticUpdates
columns.description: CSP: AllowAutoUpdate &'AuOptions'  Enables the IT admin to manage automatic update behavior to scan, download, and install updates.

columns.name: WUDeadlineNoAutoRestart
columns.description: CSP:ConfigureDeadlineNoAutoReboot. Devices will not automatically restart outside of active hours until the deadline is reached, 1 - Enabled 0 (default) - Disabled

columns.name: WUNotificationLevel
columns.description: CSP: UpdateNotificationLevel. This policy allows you to define what Windows Update notifications users see.  0 (default) � Use the default Windows Update notifications. 1 � Turn off all notifications, excluding restart warnings. 2 � Turn off all notifications, including restart warnings

columns.name: WUPauseUXDisabled
columns.description: CSP: SetDisablePauseUXAccess. This policy allows the IT admin to disable the Pause Updates feature. When this policy is enabled, the user cannot access the Pause updates" feature. Supported values 0, 1.

columns.name: WUUXDisabled
columns.description: CSP:SetDisableUXWUAccess.This policy allows the IT admin to remove access to scan Windows Update. When this policy is enabled, the user cannot access the Windows Update scan, download, and install features. Default is 0. Supported values 0, 1.

columns.name: WUServiceURLConfigured
columns.description: CSP:UpdateServiceUrl. The following list shows the supported values: Not configured. The device checks for updates from Microsoft Update. Set to a URL, such as http://abcd-srv:8530. The device checks for updates from the WSUS server at the specified URL. Not configured. The device checks for updates from Microsoft Update. Set to a URL, such as http://abcd-srv:8530. The device checks for updates from the WSUS server at the specified URL.

columns.name: WURestartNotification
columns.description: CSP: AutoRestartRequiredNotificationDismissal. Allows the IT Admin to specify the method by which the auto-restart required notification is dismissed.The following list shows the supported values:  1 (default) = Auto Dismissal. 2 � User Dismissal.

columns.name: LastWUScanTime
columns.description: The last time this device performed a successful WU Scan, if any.

columns.name: LastCensusScanTime
columns.description: The last time this device performed a successful Census Scan, if any.

columns.name: IsDeviceHotpatchEnrolled
columns.description: Indicates whether the device has been enrolled to receive windows security updates without requiring a restart.

columns.name: IsDeviceVBSEnabled
columns.description: Whether Virtual Based Security (VBS) is enabled on the device. Enabling VBS is a prerequisite for devices to recieve windows security updates without a restart.

columns.name: IsDeviceHotpatchEligible
columns.description: Specifies whether a device meets the necessary criteria to receive windows security updates without requiring a restart.

columns.name: LastHotpatchEnrolledTime
columns.description: The last time when device was enrolled to receive security updates without restart.

name: UCDeviceAlert
description: Update Compliance - These alerts are activated as a result of an issue that is device-specific, and is not specific to a specific update and a specific device. Like UpdateAlerts, the AlertType indicates where the Alert comes from (ServiceDeviceAlert, ClientDeviceAlert). For example, an EndOfService alert is a ClientDeviceAlert, as the fact it is on a build no longer being serviced (EOS) is a client-wide state. Meanwhile, DeviceRegistrationIssues in WUfB DS will be a ServiceDeviceAlert, as it is a device-wide state in the service to not be correctly registered.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Time at which this event is generated and logged.

columns.name: AlertId
columns.description: The unique identifier of this Alert.

columns.name: GlobalDeviceId
columns.description: Microsoft internal Global Device Identifier.

columns.description: A GUID corresponding to this device's AAD Device ID.

columns.name: DeviceName
columns.description: Device's given name.

columns.name: AlertType
columns.description: The type of Alert this is, ClientUpdateAlert, ServiceUpdateAlert. Indicates which fields will be present.

columns.name: AlertSubtype
columns.description: The Subtype of Alert.

columns.name: AlertStatus
columns.description: Whether this Alert is Active, Resolved, or Deleted.

columns.name: AlertRank
columns.description: Integer ranking of Alert for prioritization during troubleshooting.

columns.name: AlertClassification
columns.description: Whether this Alert is an Error, a Warning, or Informational.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: A localized string translated from a combination of other Alert fields + language preference that describes the issue in detail.

columns.name: Recommendation
columns.description: A localized string translated from RecommendedAction, Message, and other fields (depending on source of Alert) that provides a recommended action.

columns.name: AlertData
columns.description: An optional string formatted as a json payload containing metadata for the alert.

columns.name: ErrorCode
columns.description: The Error Code, if any, that triggered this Alert. In the case of Client-based explicit alerts, error codes can have extended error codes, which are appended to the error code with a underscore separator.

columns.name: ErrorSymName
columns.description: The symbolic name that maps to the Error Code, if any. Otherwise empty.

columns.name: URL
columns.description: An optional URL to get more in-depth information related to this alert.

columns.name: StartTime
columns.description: The time this alert was activated.

columns.name: ResolvedTime
columns.description: The time this alert was resolved, else empty.

name: WVDAutoscaleEvaluationPooled
description: The results of an Azure Virtual Desktop Autoscale scaling plan evaluation on a hostpool. This includes information on the actions Autoscale took on the sessions hosts, such as starting or deallocating them, and why it took those actions. The column names that start with 'Config' contain the scaling plan configuration values for the current Autoscale schedule phase. If the ResultType column value is 'Failed' then join to the WVDErrors table using the CorrelationId column to get more details. For Autoscale documentation see https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2169532 .

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) this event was generated.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: A GUID generated for this Autoscale evaluation.

columns.name: ScalingPlanResourceId
columns.description: Resource ID of the Autoscale scaling plan.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Status of this evaluation event.

columns.name: ScalingReasonMessage
columns.description: The actions Autoscale decided to perform and why it took those actions.

columns.name: ScalingEvaluationStartTime
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) when the Autoscale evaluation started.

columns.name: ConfigScheduleName
columns.description: Name of schedule used in the evaluation.

columns.name: ConfigSchedulePhase
columns.description: Schedule phase at the time of evaluation.

columns.name: MaxSessionLimitPerSessionHost
columns.description: The 'MaxSessionLimit' value defined on the host pool. The is the maximum number of user sessions allowed per session host.

columns.name: ConfigCapacityThresholdPercent
columns.description: Capacity threshold percent.

columns.name: ConfigMinActiveSessionHostsPercent
columns.description: Minimum percent of session hosts that should be active.

columns.name: TotalSessionHostCount
columns.description: Number of session hosts in the host pool.

columns.name: UnhealthySessionHostCount
columns.description: Number of session hosts in a faulty state.

columns.name: ExcludedSessionHostCount
columns.description: Number of session hosts excluded from being managed by Autoscale.

columns.name: ActiveSessionHostCount
columns.description: Number of session hosts accepting user connections.

columns.name: SessionCount
columns.description: Number of user sessions, only the user sessions from session hosts which considered active by Autoscale are included.

columns.name: SessionOccupancyPercent
columns.description: Percent of session host capacity occupied by user sessions.

columns.name: ActiveSessionHostsPercent
columns.description: Percent of session hosts in the host pool considered active by Autoscale.

columns.name: LinkId
columns.description: The identifier uses to track of network transport path.

columns.name: GatewayRegion
columns.description: The Azure region where the client’s connection gateway is located.

columns.name: ClientTransportType
columns.description: The transport protocol the AVD client is using for an individual link.

columns.name: ServerTransportType
columns.description: The transport protocol the AVD session host is using for an individual link.

columns.name: ClientNatIP
columns.description: The public IP address for the client's NAT.

columns.name: ServerNatIP
columns.description: The public ip address of the session host.

columns.name: ServerTURNIP
columns.description: The IP address of the TURN server that the AVD client connects to.

columns.name: ClientTURNIP
columns.description: The IP address of the client side used for connecting to the TURN server.

columns.name: ClientTransportIP
columns.description: The IP address of the client endpoint used for the transport connection to the session host.

columns.name: ServerTransportIP
columns.description: The IP address of the session host used for the transport connection.

name: WVDSessionHostManagement
description: Windows Virtual Desktop session host management data.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of the event.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: The correlation ID for the activity.

columns.name: ClientType
columns.description: Information about the client that initiated the update (portal, Powershell etc.).

columns.name: FailedSessionHostCleanupPolicy
columns.description: The policy for cleaning up session hosts that have failed provisioning.

columns.name: FromInstanceCount
columns.description: The instance count before the operation. For an update operation, FromInstanceCount and ToInstanceCount are the same value.

columns.name: FromSessionHostConfigVer
columns.description: The version of SHC before the operation (that session hosts are moving from; can be looked up with new SHC table). For a provisioning operation, it is the same as the ToSessionHostConfiguration.

columns.name: UpdateMaxVmsRemoved
columns.description: The maximum number of virtual machines that might become unavailable during the session host update operation.

columns.name: ProvisioningStatus
columns.description: The status of the current update/provisioning operation.

columns.name: ProvisioningType
columns.description: The type of operation (provisioning, update).

columns.name: ProvisioningCanaryPolicy
columns.description: The policy for creating a test canary session host before creating the rest of the requested session hosts.

columns.name: ScheduledDateTime
columns.description: When the session host update is scheduled, the scheduled time.

columns.name: ScheduledDateTimeZone
columns.description: The time zone that updates and provisioning happen in.

columns.name: ToInstanceCount
columns.description: The instance count after the operation. For an update operation, FromInstanceCount and ToInstanceCount are the same value.

columns.name: ToSessionHostConfigVer
columns.description: The version of SHC after the operation (that session hosts are moving to; can be looked up with new SHC table). For a provisioning operation, is the same as the FromSessionHostConfiguration is.

columns.name: UpdateMethod
columns.description: The method that is used for the session host update operation (e.g.: VmRecreate).

columns.name: UpdateStartWindowInMinutes
columns.description: The window of allowable time for an update to start in minutes.

columns.name: UpdateDeleteOriginalVm
columns.description: Property indicates whether the original VM should be deleted after the update.

name: WebPubSubConnectivity
description: Connectivity logs provide detailed information for Azure Web PubSub hub connections. For example, basic information (user ID, connection ID, and so on) and event information (connect, disconnect, and abort event, and so on) and can be used to troubleshoot connection-related issues.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of the log.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: The operation of the log event. It can be used to filter the log based on a specific operation name.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The location of Azure Web PubSub service.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: The level of the log. Can be 'Informational', 'Warning', 'Error' or 'Critical'.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: The IP of the client or server connects to Web PubSub service.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: The message of the log event. It provides details about the event.

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: The unique identifier of the user. It is defined by the client or app server.

columns.name: ConnectionId
columns.description: The unique identifier of the connection connected to service.

name: WebPubSubHttpRequest
description: Http request logs provide detailed information for the http requests received by Azure Web PubSub. For example, status code and url of the request and is helpful to troubleshoot request-related issues.

columns.name: Level
columns.description: The level of the log. Can be 'Informational', 'Warning', 'Error' or 'Critical'.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress

columns.name: Url

columns.name: DurationMs

columns.name: Headers

columns.name: TraceId

name: WebPubSubMessaging
description: Messaging logs provide tracing information for the Azure Web PubSub hub messages received and sent via Azure Web PubSub service. For example, tracing ID and message type of the message. Typically the message is recorded when it arrives at or leaves from service and is helpful for troubleshooting message-related issues.
